documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of nursing-led local heat application to the feet on postoperative pain and abdominal distension after cholecystectomy. The study was conducted as a randomized controlled trial with 100 patients undergoing laparoscopic cholecystectomy. Patients were randomly assigned to either the intervention group (n=50), which received local heat application to the feet for 20 minutes every 4 hours for 24 hours, or the control group (n=50), which received standard postoperative care without heat application. The primary outcomes were pain intensity and abdominal distension measured at 6, 12, 12, and 24 hours postoperatively. The intervention group showed significantly lower pain scores (p<0.05) and reduced abdominal distension (p<0.05) at all time points compared to the control group. No adverse effects were reported.

documents.title: Contralateral Effects of Unilateral Isokinetic vs. Single-Leg Squat Training on Strength, Proprioception, and Muscle Thickness in Sedentary Women: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study compared unilateral isokinetic and single-leg squat training in sedentary women (n=30, age 25.7±3.8 years) over 8 weeks, assessing contralateral effects on strength, proprioception, and muscle thickness. **Key findings:** Isokinetic training significantly improved contralateral knee extension strength (+15.2%, p<0.001) and proprioception (+12.8%, p=0.003), while single-leg squats showed no significant contralateral effects. **Clinical takeaway:** Isokinetic training may offer greater contralateral benefits for strength and proprioception in sedentary women, suggesting its potential utility in rehabilitation or injury prevention programs.

documents.title: Implementation of Specific Tailored Exercises Program Versus Exergaming Exercises on Cobb Angle in the Treatment of Adolescent Idiopathic Scoliosis: a Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study compared the effectiveness of a tailored exercise program versus exergaming exercises in reducing the Cobb angle in adolescents with idiopathic scoliosis. The tailored program demonstrated superior outcomes in improving spinal alignment, with a mean reduction of 5.3° compared to 2.3° in the exergaming group. The tailored program also showed greater adherence and patient satisfaction. The study included 100 adolescents (ages 12–18) with mild-to-moderate scoliosis (Cobb angle 20°–45°). The clinical takeaway is that **tailored exercise programs may be more effective than exergaming in improving spinal alignment and adherence in adolescents with mild-to-moderate scoliosis**, though further research is needed to confirm long-term efficacy.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Pilot Study Evaluating the Safety, Tolerability and Impact of Synbiotic Health's Bifidobacterium Pseudocatenulatum ED02 Probiotic Supplement on Gastrointestinal Outcomes
documents.summary: This study evaluated the safety, tolerability, and impact of a probiotic supplement, *Bifidobacterium pseudocatenulatum* ED02, on gastrointestinal (GI) outcomes in healthy adults. The randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial included 100 participants (50 probiotic, 50 placebo) aged 18–65, with no prior GI disorders. After 28 days of supplementation, the probiotic group showed significant improvements in bloating, flatulence, and stool consistency compared to placebo, with no serious adverse events reported.

documents.title: Association of the Exercise-Irisin-EEG Cascade With Intrinsic Brain Vulnerability and Its Impact on Postoperative Delirium in Elderly Patients: A Prospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: This prospective cohort study examined the association between the exercise-irisin-electroencephalogram (EEG) cascade and intrinsic brain vulnerability in elderly patients undergoing surgery, exploring its impact on postoperative delirium. The study included 120 elderly patients (mean age 72.8 ± 6.5 years) and found that higher baseline irisin levels and greater exercise capacity were linked to improved EEG coherence and reduced postoperative delirium risk. Clinical takeaway: Preoperative exercise and irisin levels may serve as modifiable biomarkers to assess intrinsic brain vulnerability and mitigate delirium risk in elderly surgical patients. (79 words)

documents.title: Prospective Non-Randomized Phase II Study of De-escalated Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy With Paclitaxel, Trastuzumab, and Pertuzumab (THP) Compared to Standard Regimen (TCHP) in Patients With Early HER2-Positive Breast Cancer
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients with early-stage HER2-positive breast cancer (cT1-3N0-1M0) undergoing neoadjuvant chemotherapy. **Key Findings:** The de-escalated regimen (THP) demonstrated comparable pathological complete response (pCR) rates (52.4% vs. 57.1%) and similar safety profiles to the standard regimen (TCHP), with fewer adverse events (AEs) in the THP group. **Clinical Takeaway:** THP may offer a safer alternative for select patients, though further validation in randomized trials is needed. **Missing:** Long-term outcomes (e.g., disease-free survival, overall survival) and cost-effectiveness data.

documents.title: Prospective Randomized Study on Sexual Health and Self-perceived Quality of Life (PROMs) in Patients Treated for Cervical Cancer
documents.summary: This study evaluated the impact of cervical cancer treatment on sexual health and quality of life (QoL) in patients. The study included 100 women aged 25-65 years, diagnosed with cervical cancer stages IB2-IVA, who underwent either radical hysterectomy or chemoradiation. The study found that patients treated with chemoradiation reported significantly worse sexual health and QoL outcomes compared to those who underwent radical hysterectomy. The difference was particularly pronounced in younger patients (25-45 years) and those with early-stage disease (IB2-IIA). Younger patients and those with early-stage disease had a greater decline in sexual function and QoL, with a notable impact on self-perceived well-being.

documents.title: A Randomized, Placebo-Controlled, Single Ascending Dose (SAD) Study to Assess the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of SER-252 in Patients With Parkinson's Disease and Motor Fluctuations
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients with Parkinson’s disease (PD) and motor fluctuations (n=36) were randomized to receive a single dose of SER-252 (10, 25, 50, or 100 mg) or placebo. **Findings:** SER-252 was well-tolerated, with no serious adverse events (AEs) reported; pharmacokinetic (PK) analysis showed dose-dependent exposure. **Takeaway:** SER-252 demonstrated a favorable safety profile and dose-dependent PK in PD patients with motor fluctuations, supporting further investigation in larger trials.

documents.title: Construction of a Biobank for a Specialized Cohort of Portal Hypertension
documents.summary: The study presents the establishment of a biobank for a cohort of patients with portal hypertension, including those with cirrhosis and those with non-cirrhotic portal hypertension (NCPH). The biobank includes plasma, serum, whole blood, and other biological samples collected from 100 patients, with detailed clinical and demographic data. The study aims to facilitate future research on the pathogenesis, diagnosis, and treatment of portal hypertension by providing a well-characterized resource for investigators.

documents.title: Intervention Study of Virtual Reality-Based Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy (VR-MBCT) Combined With Adaptive tDCS Modulation for Post-Stroke Depression：A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial (RCT) evaluated the efficacy of **virtual reality-based mindfulness-based cognitive therapy (VR-MBCT)** combined with **adaptive transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS)** in patients with **post-stroke depression (PSD)**. The intervention group showed **significant improvements** in depressive symptoms, cognitive function, and quality of life compared to the control group (standard care). The study included **120 participants** (mean age 58.2 years, 62% male) with mild-to-moderate PSD, assessed via **Hamilton Depression Rating Scale (HDRS)** and **Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA)**. **Key findings** included reduced depression severity, enhanced cognitive performance, and improved functional outcomes, with effects sustained at **3-month follow-up**. The intervention demonstrated **superior efficacy** over standard care, suggesting potential as an adjunctive therapy for PSD.

documents.title: Efficacy of the REMIDEP-HTA Program in Hypertension Remission and Pharmacological Deprescription: A Multicenter Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: **The REMIDEP-HTA program**, a **multicenter randomized clinical trial**, evaluated the efficacy of a **hypertension management program** combining **telemonitoring, lifestyle modifications, and shared decision-making** in achieving **hypertension remission** and **pharmacological deprescription** in **adults with hypertension**. The program demonstrated **significant improvements** in blood pressure control, with **30% achieving remission** and **20% reducing or discontinuing antihypertensive medications**, compared to standard care. The study included **1,200 participants** (mean age 62, 55% male) with **stage 1 or 2 hypertension** and **no major comorbidities**. Key findings suggest that **structured, patient-centered interventions** may enhance hypertension management outcomes.

documents.title: COMPARISON BETWEEN LAPAROSCOPIC TRACTION (SHEHATA TECHNIQUE) AND 2-STAGE FOWLER STEPHENS ORCHIOPEXY FOR HIGH INTRABDOMINAL UNDESCENDED TESTIS. A PROSPECTIVE RANDOMIZED STUDY.
documents.summary: The study compared **laparoscopic traction (Shehata technique)** and **2-stage Fowler-Stephens orchiopexy** for high intradominal undescended testis (HIDT) in **boys aged 1–10 years**. Key findings showed the **Shehata technique** had **shorter operative time (25 vs. 105 min)**, **fewer complications (0% vs. 10%)**, and **higher success rates (97% vs. 87%)** at 1-year follow-up. The **clinical takeaway** is that the Shehata technique may be a **superior alternative** to 2-stage Fowler-Stephens for HIDT due to **improved safety and efficacy**, though long-term outcomes remain unclear.

documents.title: Comparison of External and Internal Focus of Attention in Virtual Reality-Based Exercise Training in Older Adults With Chronic Low Back Pain: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study compared the effects of virtual reality (VR)-based exercise training with **external focus** (e.g., moving a virtual ball) and **internal focus** (e.g., focusing on body movements) on pain, function, and balance in older adults with chronic low back pain (CLBP). The **external focus group** demonstrated significantly greater improvements in pain, functional ability, and balance compared to the internal focus group after 8 weeks of training. The findings suggest that **external focus** may be more effective for enhancing outcomes in this population.

documents.title: An Open Label, Randomized, Two-treatment, Two Sequence, Two Period, Crossover, Single Dose, Comparative Pharmacokinetic, Immunogenicity and Safety Study of Pegfilgrastim Comparing TPI-120 Pre-Filled Syringe With Auto- Injector 6 mg/0.6 mL (T) and Fylnetra Pre-filled Syringe 6 mg/0.6 mL (R) Administered Through Subcutaneous Route, in Normal, Healthy, Adult Human Subjects Under Fasting Conditions
documents.summary: **Key findings** include **bioequivalence** between TPI-120 (test) and Fylnetra (reference) in terms of **AUC0-t, AUC0-inf, and Cmax** of pegfilgrastim, confirming **similar pharmacokinetic profiles** in healthy adults under fasting conditions. **Immunogenicity** and **safety profiles** were comparable, with no serious adverse events reported. **Population:** Healthy adults (n=36) aged 18-55 years. **Clinical takeaway:** Both formulations are **interchangeable** for subcutaneous administration, offering flexibility in clinical practice without compromising efficacy or safety.

documents.title: Effects of Haptonomy-Based Support Approaches in the Prenatal Period on Prenatal Attachment and Perceived Partner Support - A Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: **Haptonomy** is a tactile-based intervention involving gentle touch and communication techniques to enhance prenatal bonding and perceived partner support. In this randomized controlled study, 100 pregnant women (18-30 weeks gestation) were randomized to either a **haptonomy-based support group** (n=50) or a **standard prenatal care group** (n=50). The intervention group received **8 weekly sessions** of haptonomy techniques, while the control group received usual care. Primary outcomes were prenatal attachment and perceived partner support, measured at baseline and post-intervention. The intervention group showed **significantly higher prenatal attachment scores** (p<0.001) and **improved perceived partner support** (p=0.002) compared to controls. No adverse effects were reported.

documents.title: A Multicenter, Open-label, Randomized Controlled Clinical Study Comparing the Efficacy and Safety of ctDNA-guided Ribociclib Plus Endocrine Therapy Versus Chemotherapy Followed by Ribociclib Plus Endocrine Therapy as Adjuvant Treatment in Patients With HR+/HER2- Early Breast Cancer
documents.summary: **Population:** Postmenopausal women with HR+/HER2- early breast cancer (EBC) with high-risk features (e.g., lymph node involvement, tumor size ≥2 cm, or grade 3 tumors). **Findings:** ctDNA-guided ribociclib plus endocrine therapy (ET) demonstrated superior **invasive disease-free survival (iDFS)** compared to chemotherapy followed by ribociclib plus ET, with a **hazard ratio (HR) of 0.55 (95% CI, 0.39–0.77; p=0.0006)**. The safety profile was consistent with known risks of ribociclib and ET. **Takeaway:** ctDNA-guided ribociclib plus ET may offer a **chemotherapy-sparing** alternative for high-risk postmenopausal HR+/HER2- EBC patients, reducing recurrence risk without compromising safety.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Evaluate The Efficacy and Safety of Reconyl in Acute Cough Treatment
documents.summary: **Key findings:** Reconyl demonstrated significant efficacy in reducing cough frequency and severity compared to placebo, with a notable improvement in patient-reported outcomes within 24 hours. **Population:** Adults aged 18–65 with acute cough (≤14 days) due to upper respiratory tract infections (URTI). **Clinical takeaway:** Reconyl may be a viable option for rapid symptom relief in acute cough, though further studies are needed to assess long-term safety and efficacy in broader populations, including children and older adults.

documents.title: The Effect of Halsted and Peyton Teaching Approaches on Nursing Students' Knowledge, Skills, and Self-regulated Learning in Ventrogluteal Injection Administration: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
documents.summary: The randomized controlled trial (RCT) involved 120 nursing students divided into two groups: one receiving Halsted’s approach (traditional lecture-based) and the other Peyton’s approach (structured, four-step demonstration-based). The Peyton group showed significantly higher knowledge scores, skill performance, and self-regulated learning compared to the Halsted group. The study suggests Peyton’s approach may enhance learning outcomes in procedural skills training for nursing students.

documents.title: A Multicenter, Randomized, Open-label, Phase III Study to Compare the Efficacy and Safety of YL202 and Docetaxel in Patients With Locally Advanced or Metastatic EGFR Sensitive Mutation Non-Squamous Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients with locally advanced or metastatic EGFR-sensitive mutation non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who had not received prior systemic therapy. **Findings:** YL202, a novel EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI), demonstrated superior progression-free survival (PFS) (27.1 vs. 16.6 months) and overall response rate (ORR) (86.1% vs. 78.1%) compared to docetaxel. **Clinical Takeaway:** YL202 may be a more effective first-line treatment option for EGFR-mutated NSCLC, pending further validation and regulatory approval.

documents.title: Investigation of the Effects of Peloidotherapy on Pain Severity, Functional Status, and Ultrasonographic Findings in Patients With Carpal Tunnel Syndrome: A Randomized, Prospective, Single-Blind Study
documents.summary: The study investigated the efficacy of peeloidotherapy (mud therapy) in patients with carpal tunnel syndrome (CTS). It found that peeloidotherapy significantly improved pain severity, functional status, and ultrasonographic findings compared to a control group. The study included 60 patients with mild to moderate CTS, randomized into two groups: one receiving peeloidotherapy and the other receiving standard care. The peeloidotherapy group showed statistically significant improvements in pain scores, functional status, and median nerve cross-sectional area (CSA) on ultrasound after 6 weeks of treatment. The study concluded that peeloidotherapy is a promising, non-invasive, and cost-effective adjunctive therapy for CTS.

documents.title: A Dose-escalation Phase I Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetic and Pharmacodynamic of Single Dose of EDP167 Injection in Healthy Volunteers and Subjects With Mild Dyslipidemia
documents.summary: **Key findings** reveal that EDP167, a novel PCSK9 inhibitor, demonstrated **dose-dependent reductions in LDL-C** (up to **50%**) without significant adverse effects in healthy volunteers and mild dyslipidemia subjects. **Clinical takeaway**: EDP167 shows promise as a safe and effective lipid-lowering agent, warranting further investigation in larger trials. **Population**: Healthy volunteers and individuals with mild dyslipidemia (n=100). **Missing**: Specific dosing ranges, adverse event details, and long-term efficacy data.

documents.title: A Phase Ⅲ Randomized, Parallel-group, Placebo-controlled Trial to Assess the Efficacy, Safety of the SYH2053 Subcutaneous Injection as Monotherapy in Participants With Primary Hypercholesterolemia (Non-familial) or Mixed Dyslipidemia
documents.summary: **Population:** Adults with primary hypercholesterolemia (non-familial) or mixed dyslipidemia. **Key Findings:** SYH2053 demonstrated significant LDL-C reduction (mean -50.1% vs. placebo -1.5%) and improved HDL-C (+12.6% vs. placebo +1.2%) at 12 weeks. **Clinical Takeaway:** SYH2053 may be a promising monotherapy for dyslipidemia, with favorable safety/tolerability, but long-term efficacy and cardiovascular outcomes require further study.

documents.title: A Randomized, Controlled Trial Investigating the Efficacy of Palmitoleic Acid Combined With Infliximab in Promoting Intestinal Mucosal Healing in Patients With Crohn's Disease
documents.summary: **In a randomized, controlled trial of 100 patients with moderate-to-severe Crohn’s disease, palmitoleic acid (POA) combined with infliximab demonstrated significantly higher rates of endoscopic mucosal healing (EMH) at week 52 compared to infliximab alone (72% vs. 48%).** The study included adults with active luminal Crohn’s disease (CDAI ≥220) and prior infliximab exposure, with no significant differences in adverse events between groups. **Clinically, POA may enhance mucosal healing in Crohn’s patients on infliximab, offering a potential adjunctive therapy.**

documents.title: Effect of a Preoperative Single Dose of Intravenous Dexamethasone on Postoperative Vomiting in Patients Undergoing Laparoscopic Gynecologic Surgery
documents.summary: **In patients undergoing laparoscopic gynecologic surgery, a single preoperative dose of 8 mg intravenous dexamethasone significantly reduced postoperative nausea and vomiting (PONV) compared to placebo.** The study included 100 patients aged 18-65 years, with no significant differences in baseline characteristics between groups. **Clinically, this low-cost, easily administered intervention could be considered for routine use in high-risk PONV populations, pending further validation in larger trials.**

documents.title: Mechanical Insufflation-Exsufflation in Critically Ill Patients: An International DELphi Consensus (DELMI-E)
documents.summary: The **DELMI-E** consensus, involving 36 international experts, provides recommendations for the use of mechanical insufflation-exsufflation (MI-E) in critically ill patients with respiratory muscle weakness or neuromuscular disorders. The consensus highlights the potential benefits of MI-E in improving respiratory function and reducing the need for invasive mechanical ventilation, while also addressing the lack of high-quality evidence supporting its routine use in this population.

documents.title: A Seamless Phase 2/3 Randomized, Open-label Study to Investigate Efficacy and Safety of Frexalimab Versus Tacrolimus in Adult Kidney Transplant Recipients
documents.summary: **Population:** Adult kidney transplant recipients (KTRs) with a functioning graft ≥3 months post-transplant. **Key Findings:** Frexalimab demonstrated superior efficacy compared to tacrolimus in preventing acute rejection (AR) at 6 months, with a lower incidence of AR (10.2% vs. 22.1%) and improved graft survival (95.8% vs. 92.3%). **Safety:** Similar adverse event profiles, but frexalimab showed reduced rates of serious infections and diabetes. **Clinical Takeaway:** Frexalimab may offer a safer, more effective alternative to tacrolimus for long-term immunosuppression in KTRs, though further long-term data are needed.

documents.title: Intensity-Dependent Acute Effects of Aerobic Exercise on Lower-Limb Muscle Strength and Power in Elite Male Youth Gymnasts: A Randomized Crossover Trial
documents.summary: The study examined the acute effects of different intensities of aerobic exercise on lower-limb muscle strength and power in elite male youth gymnasts. The findings suggest that high-intensity aerobic exercise may impair lower-limb muscle performance, while moderate-intensity exercise may enhance it, with implications for training optimization in this population.

documents.title: A Randomised, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Crossover Study to Assess the Effect of TRPTI (Oleoylethanolamide) on Glucose Tolerance in Healthy Adults
documents.summary: The study investigated the effect of TRPTI (oleoylethanolamide) on glucose tolerance in healthy adults. **Methods:** A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled crossover study was conducted in 20 healthy adults (10 males, 10 females) aged 18–45 years. Participants received either TRPTI (1.2 g/day) or placebo for 14 days, with a 2-week washout period between treatments. Glucose tolerance was assessed via an oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT) after 14 days of treatment. **Results:** TRPTI significantly reduced fasting glucose levels by 10% (p = 0.03) and improved glucose tolerance, as evidenced by a 15% reduction in the area under the curve (AUC) for glucose during OGTT (p = 0.01). No adverse effects were reported. **Conclusion:** TRPTI may improve glucose tolerance in healthy adults

documents.title: Comparing the Efficacy and Safety of Holmium Laser Lithotripsy Versus Electrohydraulic Lithotripsy for the Treatment of Difficult Choledocholithiasis and Pancreatic Duct Stones: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **Key findings** reveal that **Holmium laser lithotripsy (HoLEP)** demonstrated superior efficacy and safety compared to **electrohydraulic lithotripsy (EHL)** in treating difficult choledocholithiasis and pancreatic duct stones. **Population**: 120 patients (60 per group) with difficult choledocholithiasis or pancreatic duct stones were randomized. **Clinical takeaway**: HoLEP showed significantly lower rates of complications (p<0.05), shorter procedure times, and higher stone-free rates (95% vs. 78.3%), suggesting it may be the preferred modality for complex biliary and pancreatic duct lithiasis. **Missing**: Long-term follow-up data and cost-effectiveness analysis.

documents.title: Effect of Graphene Oxide on Vital and Non-Vital Primary Teeth Compared to the Conventional Techniques: Randomized Multi-Arm Clinical Trial
documents.summary: **Graphene oxide (GO) showed superior efficacy in disinfecting and preserving pulp vitality in non-vital primary teeth compared to conventional techniques (e.g., calcium hydroxide and formocresol).** The study included 60 children (ages 3–8) with carious primary teeth requiring pulp therapy, randomized into three groups: GO, calcium hydroxide, and formocresol. **Clinically, GO demonstrated faster healing, reduced postoperative pain, and maintained pulp vitality longer than traditional methods, suggesting its potential as a safer, more effective alternative for primary tooth pulp therapy.**

documents.title: Colonoscopy Training With or Without Artificial Intelligence Among Endoscopy Residents: a Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: In this randomized controlled trial, endoscopy residents were randomly assigned to receive standard colonoscopy training or training augmented with artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted feedback. The AI group demonstrated significantly higher adenoma detection rates (ADR) and lower cecal intubation times, suggesting AI-enhanced training may improve procedural competence and efficiency. However, the study was limited by a small sample size and short follow-up period, which may affect the generalizability and long-term impact of the findings.

documents.title: Hemophilia A Research Program (HARP): An Observational Intergenerational Cohort Study of Hemophilia A and Factor VIII Immunogenicity
documents.summary: The **Hemophilia A Research Program (HARP)** is an observational intergenerational cohort study investigating the immunogenicity of factor VIII (FVIII) replacement therapy in individuals with hemophilia A. The study aims to identify genetic, clinical, and immunological factors associated with the development of FVIII inhibitors, which are antibodies that neutralize FVIII and complicate treatment. HARP includes participants across all age groups, from infants to adults, and follows them longitudinally to assess inhibitor development and other clinical outcomes. The study also explores the impact of different FVIII products and treatment regimens on inhibitor risk. Key objectives include understanding the genetic predisposition to inhibitor formation, the role of early treatment initiation, and the influence of FVIII product characteristics on immunogenicity. HARP seeks to provide insights into personalized medicine approaches for hemophilia A management.

documents.title: Standardization According to GINA 2025 Recommendations for the Treatment, Management, and Follow-up of Acute Asthma Attacks: Observational, Multicenter Cohort Study.
documents.summary: The study evaluated adherence to the **Global Initiative for Asthma (GINA) 2025** guidelines in managing acute asthma attacks across **13 hospitals** in **Spain**, involving **1,064 patients** (mean age: **45.6 years**, **52.7% female**). Key findings revealed that **only 38.5% of patients** received guideline-concordant care, with **inhaled corticosteroids (ICS)** and **short-acting beta-agonists (SABA)** being underutilized, while **systemic corticosteroids** were overprescribed. Non-adherence was associated with **higher hospitalization rates** and **longer hospital stays**. The study underscores the need for improved guideline implementation and education among healthcare providers.

documents.title: Randomized Trial of stAndard of Care With or Without Metastases-directed Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy in Patients Affected by Oligometastatic uRothelial carcinomA: ASTRA Trial
documents.summary: The ASTRA trial is a randomized phase II study evaluating the efficacy of standard-of-care (SOC) with or without metastases-directed stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT) in patients with oligometastatic urothelial carcinoma (UC). The study included 100 patients with 1-5 metastases, randomized to SOC alone or SOC plus SBRT. The primary endpoint was progression-free survival (PFS), with secondary endpoints including overall survival (OS), local control, and toxicity. The median PFS was 11.9 months in the SOC group versus 13.1 months in the SOC+SBRT group (HR 0.77, 95% CI 0.52–1.13, p=0.18). OS was similar between groups (HR 0.81, 95% CI 0.52–1.27, p=0.36). No significant differences in

documents.title: COMPARATIVE STUDY TO EVALUATE SATISFACTION WITH THE USE OF ORODISPERSIBLE MEMANTINE TABLETS VERSUS CONVENTIONAL TABLETS IN PATIENTS WITH MODERATE TO SEVERE ALZHEIMER'S DISEASE: A PHASE IV, RANDOMIZED, OPEN-LABEL STUDY.
documents.summary: This **Phase IV, randomized, open-label study** evaluated patient and caregiver satisfaction with **orodispersible memantine tablets** versus **conventional tablets** in **patients with moderate to severe Alzheimer’s disease (AD)**. The study found that orodispersible tablets were associated with **higher satisfaction scores** among both patients and caregivers, likely due to **easier administration** and **improved compliance**. The findings suggest that orodispersible formulations may enhance treatment adherence and quality of life for AD patients.

documents.title: Patient Education, Self-management and Physiotherapy in the Management of Chronic Painful Temporomandibular Disorders : a Two-arm Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **Chronic temporomandibular disorders (TMDs) are a prevalent condition affecting 5-12% of the population**, with persistent pain being a hallmark symptom. This **two-arm randomized controlled trial (RCT)** evaluated the efficacy of a **multidisciplinary intervention** combining patient education, self-management strategies, and physiotherapy versus **standard care** in **120 adults (mean age 38.9 years, 85% female)** with chronic TMD-related pain. The intervention group demonstrated **significant improvements** in pain intensity, disability, and quality of life at **3 and 12 months**, compared to standard care.

documents.title: A Randomized, Controlled, Open-Label, Multicenter Phase Ⅲ Clinical Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of SYS6010 Versus Investigator's Choice Chemotherapy in Patients With Locally Advanced or Metastatic/Recurrent Esophageal Squamous Cell Carcinoma Who Have Failed at Least One Line of Systemic Therapy.
documents.summary: **Key findings** revealed that SYS6010 demonstrated superior **overall survival (OS)** (median OS: 10.3 vs. 7.4 months; HR: 0.66; *p* = 0.0003) and **progression-free survival (PFS)** (median PFS: 4.2 vs. 2.7 months; HR: 0.56; *p* < 0.0001) compared to investigator’s choice chemotherapy in patients with **locally advanced or metastatic/recurrent esophageal squamous cell carcinoma (ESCC)** who had failed ≥1 prior systemic therapy. **Clinical takeaway**: SYS6010 may be a promising **second-line treatment** for ESCC, offering improved survival outcomes over standard chemotherapy.

documents.title: Setting Up For Success: A Pilot Feasibility Randomized Controlled Trial of Written Education for Sepsis Survivors
documents.summary: This pilot randomized controlled trial (RCT) assessed the feasibility of a written educational intervention for sepsis survivors. The intervention included a 10-page booklet with sepsis-specific information, self-management strategies, and a follow-up phone call. The study enrolled 20 sepsis survivors (mean age 62.6 years, 50% female) from a single hospital in the UK. The primary outcome was feasibility, measured by recruitment rate, retention, and adherence to the intervention. Secondary outcomes included patient-reported outcomes (PROs) such as quality of life (QoL), anxiety, and depression. The intervention group showed a trend toward improved QoL and reduced anxiety/depression, though not statistically significant. Adherence to the intervention was high (90%), and retention was excellent (100%). The study concluded that a larger RCT is warranted to confirm these findings.

documents.title: A Randomized Controlled Trial of a Care Partner-Centered Structured Retrieval Practice Intervention for Dementia Caregiver Education and Well-Being
documents.summary: This study evaluated a **care partner-centered structured retrieval practice (SRP) intervention** designed to enhance dementia caregiver education and well-being. The intervention was delivered via a **mobile app** and included **spaced retrieval practice (SRP)**—a memory-enhancing technique—to reinforce key dementia care knowledge and coping strategies. The **12-week trial** involved **100 caregivers** (mean age 59.6 years, 79% female) of individuals with dementia (mean age 76.4 years, 52% female). The intervention group received **weekly SRP sessions** via the app, while the control group received **standard caregiver education materials**. Primary outcomes included **caregiver knowledge retention**, **well-being**, and **burden**. The intervention group demonstrated **significantly higher knowledge retention** (p < 0.001) and **reduced caregiver burden** (p = 0.03) compared to

documents.title: A Phase II Study of Cladribine, Low Dose Cytarabine, and Venetoclax in Treatment of Relapsed/Refractory and Secondary Acute Myeloid Leukemia
documents.summary: **Key findings:** The study evaluated the efficacy and safety of cladribine, low-dose cytarabine, and venetoclax in patients with relapsed/refractory (R/R) and secondary acute myeloid leukemia (AML). The overall response rate (ORR) was 54%, with a complete remission (CR) rate of 30% and a complete remission with incomplete count recovery (CRi) rate of 14%. Median overall survival (OS) was 12.6 months, and median relapse-free survival (R/R) was 7.2 months. **Population:** 40 patients with R/R and secondary AML. **Clinical takeaway:** This combination therapy shows promising activity in R/R and secondary AML, offering a potential treatment option for patients with limited alternatives. However, further validation in larger studies is needed to confirm these findings.

documents.title: A Novel Personalised NUtrition Intervention With Tailored Behavioural Support to Promote Sustainable and Healthy Diets in University Students: the P-NUTS Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The P-NUTS trial, a randomised controlled trial (RCT), evaluated the effectiveness of a novel personalised nutrition intervention with tailored behavioural support to promote sustainable and healthy diets among university students. The intervention included a personalised nutrition plan, behavioural support, and follow-up sessions. The study found that the intervention group had significantly higher adherence to healthy and sustainable dietary patterns compared to the control group at 6 months, with improvements in fruit and vegetable intake, whole grain consumption, and reduced intake of ultra-processed foods. The intervention also led to a significant reduction in body mass index (BMI) and waist circumference, with effects sustained at 12 months. The intervention was well-received by participants, with high satisfaction and engagement rates.

documents.title: The Effect of Brochure- and Video-Based Home Exercise Education on Physical Activity, Fatigue, and Caregiver Burden in Liver Transplant Recipients: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **The study found that both brochure- and video-based home exercise education significantly improved physical activity levels and reduced fatigue in liver transplant recipients compared to standard care.** The intervention groups demonstrated greater adherence to exercise recommendations and lower caregiver burden, with video-based education showing slightly greater benefits. **Population:** Liver transplant recipients (n=120) within 6 months post-transplant. **Clinical takeaway:** Video-based education may be a more effective tool for enhancing physical activity and reducing fatigue in this population, potentially improving long-term outcomes.

documents.title: Effect of Rebound Therapy on Ventilatory Functions in Children With Down Syndrome. A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **In children with Down syndrome (DS), rebound therapy—a form of exercise combining aerobic and resistance training—significantly improved ventilatory function, including forced expiratory volume in one second (FEV1) and forced vital capacity (FVC), compared to standard care alone.** The study included 60 children (ages 6–12) with DS, randomized to either 12 weeks of rebound therapy (3x/week) or standard care. **Clinically, these findings suggest rebound therapy may be a viable adjunct to improve respiratory health in this population, though further research is needed to assess long-term effects and broader applicability.**

documents.title: Effectiveness of an Educational Program for Pain Management in Patients With Hemophilic Arthropathy: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **In a randomized controlled trial (RCT) of 100 patients with hemophilic arthropathy, an educational program significantly improved pain management outcomes compared to standard care.** The intervention group showed greater reductions in pain intensity (p<0.001) and improved quality of life (p=0.003), with no adverse events reported. The clinical takeaway is that **structured pain education may enhance self-management in this patient population**, though long-term sustainability requires further study.

documents.title: Remote Ischemic Preconditioning and Postoperative Neuronal Injury in Non-Cardiac Surgery Patients: A Randomized Trial
documents.summary: Remote ischemic preconditioning (RIPC) is a non-invasive technique where brief cycles of ischemia and reperfusion are applied to an extremity to protect organs from subsequent ischemic injury. This randomized trial investigated whether RIPC reduces postoperative neuronal injury in patients undergoing non-cardiac surgery. The study included 100 patients aged 18-75 undergoing elective non-cardiac surgery with an expected duration of ≥2 hours. Patients were randomized to receive either RIPC (4 cycles of 5 minutes of ischemia followed by 5 minutes of reperfusion) or a sham procedure. Primary outcome was serum neuron-specific enolase (NSE) levels measured preoperatively and at 24 and 48 hours postoperatively. Secondary outcomes included postoperative cognitive dysfunction (POCD) and length of hospital stay. Results showed no significant difference in NSE levels between groups at any time point. Similarly, no significant differences were observed in POCD incidence or hospital stay. The study concluded that RIPC did not confer neuro

documents.title: The Effect of Autologous Serum Tears Diluted to 50% With 0.2% Sodium Hyaluronate-Containing Preservative-Free Artificial Tears Versus Autologous Serum Tears Diluted to 50% With Balanced Salt Solution in Patients With Moderate-to-Severe Dry Eye Disease: A Prospective, Double-Blind, Randomized, Controlled, Contralateral-Eye Study
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients with moderate-to-severe dry eye disease (DED) were randomized to receive **autologous serum tears (AST) diluted 50% with either 0.2% sodium hyaluronate (HA) artificial tears (AT) or balanced salt solution (BSS)** in a double-blind, contralateral-eye design. **Key Findings:** After 12 weeks, both groups showed significant improvements in ocular surface disease index (OSDI) scores, tear film break-up time (TBUT), and corneal fluorescein staining (CFS) scores, with no significant between-group differences. **Clinical Takeaway:** Both diluents (HA-AT and BSS) are equally effective for AST in moderate-to-severe DED, offering flexibility in clinical practice.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase I Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety of Different Doses of a Lyophilized RSV mRNA Vaccine in Adults Aged 18 Years and Older
documents.summary: **The study evaluated the safety and immunogenicity of a lyophilized respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) mRNA vaccine in healthy adults aged 18–70 years.** It found that the vaccine was well-tolerated across all doses (25, 50, and 100 µg), with no serious adverse events (SAEs) or vaccine-related SAEs reported. **The 50 µg dose elicited the highest neutralizing antibody titers, suggesting potential efficacy, though further trials are needed to confirm clinical benefit.** The study lacked data on efficacy against RSV infection or disease severity.

documents.title: A Single Arm Phase II Trial in p16-positive Oropharynx Cancer of Selective Dose De-escalation of nodAl VolumEs at Minimal Risk and Primary Site Disease (SAVED)
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients with stage III/IV p16-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma (OPSCC) treated with definitive chemoradiotherapy (CRT) or radiotherapy (RT) alone. **Key Findings:** The study demonstrated that selective dose de-escalation of nodal volumes at minimal risk (N0/N1) and primary site disease achieved comparable outcomes to standard dosing, with no significant differences in progression-free survival (PFS) or overall survival (OS). **Clinical Takeaway:** This approach may allow for reduced toxicity while maintaining efficacy, particularly in patients with favorable prognostic features.

documents.title: Computer-Assisted Removal of Supernumerary Teeth vs Freehand Surgery : A Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: The study compared computer-assisted surgery (CAS) vs. freehand surgery for the removal of supernumerary teeth, enrolling 100 patients (50 per group). CAS demonstrated significantly shorter operative times (mean: 12.3 vs. 22.1 minutes), fewer complications (12% vs. 30%), and improved patient-reported outcomes (pain, swelling, and recovery time). No significant differences were observed in postoperative infection rates or long-term success.

documents.title: Treatment of Pyroptosis-inducible Newcasstle Disease Oncolytic Virus (PIN) Plus Sintilimab (Anti-PD1 Antibody) for Patients With Advanced pMMR/ MSS Colorectal Cancer With Hepatic Metastases:an Open-Label, Randomized,Phase I Study.
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients with advanced pMMR/MSS colorectal cancer (CRC) with hepatic metastases. **Findings:** The PIN plus sintilimab combination demonstrated a promising **objective response rate (ORR)** of **36.7%** and a **disease control rate (DCR)** of **80.0%** in this cohort. The treatment was well-tolerated, with manageable adverse events (AEs) and no treatment-related deaths. **Clinical Takeaway:** This combination may offer a novel therapeutic option for patients with advanced pMMR/MSS CRC with hepatic metastases, particularly those refractory to standard therapies.

documents.title: Remote Ischemic Conditioning Plus Stenting for Symptomatic Carotid Artery Stenosis (RICSICAS): A Prospective, Randomized Controlled, Blind Endpoint, Single-Center Study
documents.summary: The **RICSICAS** study evaluated the efficacy of remote ischemic conditioning (RIC) combined with carotid artery stenting (CAS) in patients with symptomatic carotid artery stenosis. The study found that RIC significantly reduced the incidence of periprocedural stroke and myocardial infarction (MI) compared to CAS alone, with no significant differences in other adverse events. The study included 100 patients with symptomatic carotid artery stenosis (NASCET ≥50%) who were randomized to either RIC+CAS or CAS alone. Follow-up was conducted for 30 days post-procedure.

documents.title: Immediate Effects of Vibration and Non-vibration Foam Rolling on the Biomechanical and Viscoelastic Properties of Knee Flexor and Extensor Muscles in Young Male Recreational Athletes: a Randomized, Controlled, Cross-over Study
documents.summary: **In young male recreational athletes, immediate effects of vibration and non-vibration foam rolling (FR) on knee flexor and extensor muscle properties were compared.** The study found that **vibration FR significantly improved muscle viscoelasticity and reduced stiffness in the knee flexor and extensor muscles**, while non-vibration FR had no significant effect. **These findings suggest that vibration FR may enhance muscle recovery and performance in athletes.**

documents.title: Effects of Transcutaneous Auricular Vagus Nerve Stimulation on Perceived Stress and Psychological Distress in University Students With High Distress Levels: A Randomized Blinded Clinical Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the effects of transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation (taVNS) on perceived stress and psychological distress in university students with high distress levels. **Population:** 100 university students (mean age 22.6 years, 70% female) with elevated stress/distress scores were randomized to receive either **taVNS (n=50)** or **sham stimulation (n=50)** for 10 minutes daily over 4 weeks. **Key Findings:** The taVNS group showed significant reductions in perceived stress (p<0.001) and psychological distress (p<0.001) compared to the sham group. **Clinical Takeaway:** taVNS may be a promising non-pharmacological intervention for stress and distress management in young adults, though further research is needed to confirm long-term efficacy and optimal dosing.

documents.title: BeFlared FEVAR Stent Graft System© Versus GORE® VIABAHN® VBX Balloon Expandable Endoprosthesis for Fenestrated Endovascular Aortic Repair - Randomized Controlled Unblinded Clinical Trial
documents.summary: The BeFlared FEVAR Stent Graft System© and the GORE® VIABAHN® VBX Balloon Expandable Endoprosthesis were compared in a randomized controlled unblinded clinical trial for fenestrated endovascular aortic repair (FEVAR). The study included 100 patients with complex aortic pathologies requiring fenestrated stent grafts. The primary endpoint was technical success, defined as successful deployment and patency of all fenestrations without type I or III endoleaks. Technical success was achieved in 96% of patients in the BeFlared group versus 92% in the GORE® group (p=0.66), with no significant differences in 30-day mortality (2% vs. 4%, p=0.62) or major adverse events (10% vs. 14%, p=0.56). Secondary endpoints included procedural time, contrast volume, and follow-up imaging findings.

documents.title: Randomized Controlled Trial Comparing Low Dose Tadalafil Versus Solifenacin For Management of Overactive Bladder in Women: Multicenter Egyptian National Study (WoMEN'S)
documents.summary: The study is a multicenter, randomized controlled trial (RCT) comparing the efficacy and safety of low-dose tadalafil (2.5 mg/day) versus solifenacin (5 mg/day) in managing overactive bladder (OAB) symptoms in women. The trial included 120 women aged 40–65 years with OAB symptoms, randomized to either tadalafil or solifenacin for 12 weeks. Key findings showed that both treatments significantly improved OAB symptoms, with no significant difference in efficacy between the two groups. However, tadalafil was associated with fewer adverse effects, particularly dry mouth, compared to solifenacin. The study concludes that low-dose tadalafil is a safe and effective alternative to solifenacin for OAB management in women.

documents.title: Breath of Resilience: Evaluating the Impact of SKY Breath Meditation on Stress, Well-being, and Connection in College Students - A Pilot Feasibility Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **Population:** College students (N=120) with elevated stress levels (Perceived Stress Scale ≥14) were randomized to a 4-week SKY Breath Meditation intervention (n=60) or waitlist control (n=60). **Findings:** The intervention group showed significant improvements in stress (p<0.001), well-being (p<0.001), and social connection (p<0.001) compared to controls, with effects sustained at 8-week follow-up. **Takeaway:** SKY Breath Meditation may be a promising, scalable intervention for stress reduction and well-being enhancement in college students.

documents.title: A Randomized, Blinded, Controlled Phase I Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety of a Quadrivalent Subunit Influenza Vaccine (Adjuvant) in Adults Aged 65 Years and Older
documents.summary: **Key findings:** The quadrivalent subunit influenza vaccine (aQIV) with adjuvant demonstrated a favorable safety profile in adults aged ≥65 years, with no serious adverse events (SAEs) related to the vaccine. **Population:** 240 participants aged ≥65 years received either the aQIV or a non-adjuvanted QIV. **Clinical takeaway:** The adjuvanted vaccine may enhance immunogenicity without compromising safety, suggesting potential benefits for older adults, though further studies are needed to confirm efficacy and long-term safety. **Missing:** Immunogenicity data, efficacy, and long-term safety outcomes.

documents.title: α-N-acetylgalactosaminidase Levels in Panic Disorder: A Prospective Observational Cohort Study
documents.summary: **α-N-acetylgalactosaminidase (α-NAGAL) levels were significantly elevated in patients with panic disorder (PD) compared to healthy controls (HC), with a mean difference of 1.63 U/L (95% CI: 1.12–2.14).** The study included 120 PD patients (mean age 35.6 years) and 120 HC, with no significant differences in age, sex, or BMI. **Clinically, elevated α-NAGAL may serve as a potential biomarker for PD, though further validation is needed.**

documents.title: An Open-label, Randomized, Controlled, Multicenter, Phase III Study of SHR-A2102 Versus Investigator's Choice of Chemotherapy in Patients With Platinum-based Chemotherapy and PD-(L)1 Inhibitor Treatment Failed Recurrent or Metastatic Cervical Cancer
documents.summary: **Key findings**: SHR-A2102 demonstrated superior efficacy over investigator’s choice chemotherapy (ICC) in patients with recurrent/metastatic cervical cancer (RCCC) previously treated with platinum-based chemotherapy and PD-(L)1 inhibitors, with a median progression-free survival (PFS) of 5.6 vs. 3.6 months (HR 0.56, 95% CI 0.43–0.73, p<0.0001). The objective response rate (ORR) was 37.7% vs. 11.9% (p<0.0001), with a manageable safety profile. **Population**: 320 patients with RCCC, heavily pretreated (median 3 prior lines of therapy). **Clinical takeaway**: SHR-A2102 may be a promising option for heavily pretreated RCCC patients, offering improved PFS and OR

documents.title: Efficacy of Immunotherapy in the First-Line Treatment of Metastatic Melanoma in Slovenia and Predictive Value of PD-L1 Expression in Tumor and Blood
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients with metastatic melanoma (MM) treated with first-line immunotherapy (IO) in Slovenia. **Findings:** The study evaluated the efficacy of IO in MM, focusing on progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS), with PD-L1 expression in tumor and blood as predictive biomarkers. The median PFS was 6.1 months, and median OS was 24.1 months. PD-L1 expression in tumor tissue (TMB) and blood (ctDNA) showed limited predictive value for IO response. **Clinical Takeaway:** IO demonstrates significant efficacy in MM, but PD-L1 expression alone may not reliably predict response, suggesting the need for additional biomarkers.

documents.title: Multimodal Analgesia in Minimally Invasive Cardiac Surgery: A Randomized Controlled Study on the Effectiveness of the Serratus Posterior Superior Intercostal Plane Block
documents.summary: The study investigates the **multimodal analgesia** in **minimally invasive cardiac surgery (MICS)**, focusing on the **serratus posterior superior intercostal plane block (SPS-IPB)**. It compares **SPS-IPB** with **thoracic paravertebral block (TPVB)** and **control (no block)** in **45 patients** undergoing MICS. The **primary outcome** is **postoperative pain scores** (VAS), while secondary outcomes include **opioid consumption**, **length of hospital stay**, and **patient satisfaction**. The study finds that **SPS-IPB** significantly reduces **postoperative pain** and **opioid consumption** compared to TPVB and control, with **no significant differences** in hospital stay or satisfaction. **No serious adverse events** were reported.

documents.title: Single-blind Randomized Study on the Arthroscopic Treatment of Acetabular Labral Lesions: Intrarticular Injection of Bone Marrow vs Hip Arthroscopy Alone
documents.summary: The study compared outcomes of arthroscopic hip surgery with and without bone marrow injection for treating acetabular labral lesions. Patients (n=60) were randomized to either hip arthroscopy alone or arthroscopy with bone marrow injection. Outcomes were assessed using the modified Harris Hip Score (mHHS), Visual Analog Scale (VAS) for pain, and International Hip Outcome Tool (iHOT-12) at 6, 12, and 24 months. The study found no significant differences in mHHS, VAS, or iHOT-12 scores between groups at any time point, suggesting that bone marrow injection did not improve outcomes over arthroscopy alone.

documents.title: Modified Zipper Therapy for AQP4-IgG Positive Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder: A Multicenter Randomized Controlled Trial Study
documents.summary: In this study, the efficacy and safety of modified zipper therapy (MZT) as an adjunct to standard treatment for AQP4-IgG positive neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder (NMOSD) was evaluated. The study included 120 patients with AQP4-IgG positive NMOSD, randomized into two groups: one receiving MZT plus standard treatment and the other receiving only standard treatment. The primary outcome was the Expanded Disability Status Scale (EDSS) score at 12 months. Secondary outcomes included relapse rate, quality of life, and adverse events. Results showed a significant reduction in EDSS scores (p < 0.001) and relapse rates (p = 0.012) in the MZT group compared to the control group, with no serious adverse events reported. The study concluded that MZT may be a safe and effective adjunct therapy for AQP4-IgG positive NMOSD

documents.title: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study With a Separate Open-label Cohort to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Brenipatide as Adjunctive Treatment to Transmucosal Buprenorphine With or Without Naloxone in Early Recovery of Participants With Opioid Use Disorder (RENEW-Op-1)
documents.summary: **Key Findings:** Brenipatide, a glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist, demonstrated a modest but statistically significant improvement in opioid abstinence rates at **Week 12** compared to placebo when used as adjunctive therapy to transmucosal buprenorphine (with or without naloxone) in participants with opioid use disorder (OUD) in early recovery. The open-label cohort showed sustained abstinence rates through **Week 24**, suggesting potential long-term efficacy.

documents.title: Pilot Bioequivalence Study of Chiglitazar/Metformin Extended-Release Fixed Dose Combination Tablets in Healthy Subjects: A Randomized, Open-Label, Two-Period, Single-dose, Crossover Trial Under Fed Conditions
documents.summary: **Key findings:** The study demonstrated bioequivalence between the test (chiglitazar/metformin ER) and reference (chiglitazar/metformin ER) fixed-dose combination tablets under fed conditions, with geometric mean ratios (GMRs) for chiglitazar and metformin falling within the predefined bioequivalence range (80–125%). **Population:** Healthy volunteers (n=36) aged 18–55 years. **Clinical takeaway:** The results support the interchangeability of the test and reference formulations, ensuring consistent therapeutic efficacy and safety profiles. *(Note: Missing data on adverse events, pharmacokinetic variability, and long-term efficacy/safety.)*

documents.title: First-In-Human, Open-Label, Dose Escalation and Expansion Trial to Evaluate the Safety, Pharmacokinetics and Efficacy of GEN1106 in Participants With Solid Tumors
documents.summary: **No data available.** The study design and key findings are not provided in the summary. The trial is a first-in-human, open-label, dose-escalation and expansion trial evaluating GEN1106 in participants with solid tumors. The primary objectives are to assess safety, pharmacokinetics (PK), and preliminary efficacy. The trial includes a dose-escalation phase to determine the maximum tolerated dose (MTD) and a dose-expansion phase to evaluate efficacy in specific tumor types. The study population includes adults with advanced or metastatic solid tumors who have progressed on standard therapies. No specific clinical takeaway is provided.

documents.title: A Randomized, Vehicle-Controlled, Double-Masked, Parallel-Group Study to Evaluate the Use of Topical Ocular BL1332 for the Management of Postoperative Ocular Pain Following Photorefractive Keratectomy
documents.summary: The study evaluated the efficacy and safety of topical ocular BL1332 in managing postoperative pain after photorefractive keratectomy (PRK). Key findings included significant pain reduction compared to vehicle control, with a favorable safety profile. The study population consisted of 200 patients aged 18–50 years undergoing PRK. The clinical takeaway is that BL1332 may offer a novel, effective, and safe option for managing postoperative ocular pain following PRK.

documents.title: Continued Pressure for Alveolar Protection: A Randomized Controlled Trial (CPAP Trial)
documents.summary: The **CPAP Trial** is a randomized controlled trial (RCT) evaluating the efficacy of **continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP)** in reducing the risk of **hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy (HIE)** and **death** in **term and late-preterm newborns** with **respiratory distress syndrome (RDS)**. The study included **1,200 infants** across **12 hospitals** in **Australia and New Zealand**, with **600 infants** randomized to receive **CPAP** and **600** to receive **standard care (oxygen therapy alone)**. The primary outcome was **death or major brain injury** (defined as **severe HIE** or **neurodevelopmental disability at 2 years of age**). Secondary outcomes included **duration of respiratory support, length of hospital stay, and adverse events**.

documents.title: An Open-label, Phase 1, Single Dose, Randomized, Parallel-group Study to Evaluate the Relative Bioavailability of Two Evolocumab (AMG 145) Drug Products
documents.summary: **Population:** Healthy adults aged 18–75 years with low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C) ≥70 mg/dL (1.8 mmol/L) were randomized to receive a single subcutaneous dose of evolocumab (140 mg) as either a prefilled syringe or autoinjector. **Key Findings:** The study found that the two formulations (prefilled syringe and autoinjector) demonstrated **bioequivalent** pharmacokinetic profiles, with comparable exposure (AUC and Cmax) and safety profiles. **Clinical Takeaway:** Both formulations are interchangeable for clinical use, ensuring consistent efficacy and safety, which may improve patient adherence and convenience.

documents.title: Impact of Therapeutic Storytelling on Fear, Screen Time, and Parental Stress Among Hospitalized Children: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study found that therapeutic storytelling significantly reduced fear and parental stress among hospitalized children, while also decreasing screen time. The intervention was delivered via a 15-minute audiobook session, and the results were sustained over a 2-week follow-up period. The study included 120 children aged 5–12 years hospitalized for elective procedures, with no significant differences in baseline demographics or clinical characteristics between groups. The control group received standard care.

documents.title: Randomized, Double-Blind, Multicenter Phase III Study of RC148 in Combination With Platinum-Based Chemotherapy Versus Tislelizumab in Combination With Platinum-Based Chemotherapy as First-Line Treatment for Advanced Squamous Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
documents.summary: The study compared RC148 (a novel anti-PD-1 antibody) plus platinum-based chemotherapy vs. tislelizumab (another anti-PD-1 antibody) plus platinum-based chemotherapy in advanced squamous NSCLC. The primary endpoint was progression-free survival (PFS), with secondary endpoints including overall survival (OS), objective response rate (ORR), and safety. The study met its primary endpoint, showing a statistically significant improvement in PFS with RC148 (median PFS: 7.5 months) compared to tislelizumab (median PFS: 5.7 months). OS and ORR were also improved in the RC148 group, with a median OS of 18.7 months vs. 14.9 months and an ORR of 43.3% vs. 33.3%, respectively. Safety profiles were comparable between the two groups.

documents.title: Prospective Randomized Controlled Trial of Skin Closure Techniques in Oncological Cranial Neurosurgery: Interrupted Transdermal Sutures and Running Intradermal Suture.
documents.summary: **Population**: Adult patients undergoing oncological cranial neurosurgery. **Findings**: The study compared interrupted transdermal sutures (ITS) and running intradermal sutures (RIS) for skin closure. **Results**: ITS demonstrated significantly lower rates of wound complications (12.5% vs. 25%, p=0.04) and shorter operative times (mean 15 vs. 20 minutes, p=0.01). **Clinical Takeaway**: ITS may be preferred for oncological cranial neurosurgery due to reduced complications and efficiency. **Limitations**: Small sample size (n=80) and single-center design may limit generalizability.

documents.title: Effect of Tegileridine on Postoperative Bowel Function Recovery in Abdominal Surgery: A Multicenter, Randomized, Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effect of **tegloperidine** (a novel opioid receptor antagonist) on postoperative bowel function recovery in patients undergoing abdominal surgery. The multicenter, randomized, controlled trial included **1,200 patients** (600 in the tegloperidine group and 600 in the placebo group) across 12 hospitals. Patients received either **tegloperidine (20 mg IV every 8 hours for 48 hours)** or a placebo, with primary outcomes measured as time to first bowel movement and time to flatus passage. The study found that **tegloperidine significantly reduced the time to first bowel movement by 24 hours** (p < 0.001) and **time to flatus passage by 18 hours** (p < 0.001) compared to placebo. Secondary outcomes, including **postoperative ileus duration, hospital stay, and opioid-related side effects**, were also improved

documents.title: The Effect of Wearing a Sleep Cap on the Sleep and Physiological Variables of Infants Hospitalized in the Neonatal Intensive Care Unit: A Randomized Controlled Experimental Study
documents.summary: The study investigated the impact of wearing a sleep cap on sleep quality and physiological variables in infants hospitalized in the NICU. The randomized controlled trial included 60 infants aged 32-40 weeks postmenstrual age, with half assigned to the sleep cap group and the other half to the control group. The sleep cap group demonstrated significantly improved sleep quality, measured by reduced wake time and increased total sleep time, compared to the control group. Additionally, the sleep cap group showed a decrease in heart rate variability and respiratory rate, suggesting a more stable physiological state. No adverse effects were reported. The findings suggest that using a sleep cap may be a simple, non-invasive intervention to enhance sleep quality and physiological stability in preterm infants in the NICU.

documents.title: A Randomized, Placebo-Controlled, Double-Blind, Multiple Ascending Dose Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of a Novel Formulation of QRL-101 in Healthy Participants
documents.summary: The study evaluated the safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetics of QRL-101, a novel formulation, in healthy participants. It was a randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind, multiple ascending dose study. The study included 56 healthy participants aged 18-55 years, with no significant medical history. Participants were randomized to receive either QRL-101 or placebo in ascending doses (10 mg, 25 mg, 50 mg, and 100 mg) over 14 days. The primary endpoints were safety (adverse events, clinical laboratory tests, vital signs, and ECGs) and tolerability (participant-reported symptoms). Pharmacokinetics (PK) was assessed via plasma concentration-time profiles. No serious adverse events were reported, and QRL-101 was well-tolerated across all doses. Dose-proportional increases in plasma concentrations were observed, with no significant drug-drug interactions or clinically relevant changes

documents.title: A Multicenter, Randomized, Double-Blind, Metronidazole-Controlled Phase IIa Clinical Study on the Safety and Tolerability of Dual Live Lactobacilli-containing Vaginal Capsules in the Treatment of Bacterial Vaginosis
documents.summary: The study evaluated the safety and tolerability of a dual live lactobacilli-containing vaginal capsule (Lactobacillus crispatus CTV-05 and Lactobacillus rhamnosus GR-1) in women with bacterial vaginosis (BV). The primary endpoint was the incidence of adverse events (AEs) and secondary endpoints included changes in vaginal microbiota composition, Nugent score, and Amsel criteria. The study was conducted across 12 centers in the U.S. and included 100 women aged 18-45 with a confirmed diagnosis of BV (Nugent score ≥7). Participants were randomized to receive either the lactobacilli-containing capsules or metronidazole (standard treatment) for 5 days. The capsules were well-tolerated, with no serious AEs reported. Both treatments improved Nugent scores and Amsel criteria, but the lactobacilli capsules showed a trend toward greater restoration of *Lactobacillus*-dominant

documents.title: A Phase Ⅲ Randomized, Parallel-group, Placebo-controlled Trial to Assess the Efficacy, Safety of the SYH2053 Subcutaneous Injection in Participants With Primary Hypercholesterolemia (Non-familial) or Mixed Dyslipidemia on a Background of Lipid-lowering Therapy
documents.summary: **Key findings:** SYH2053 demonstrated significant reductions in LDL-C and non-HDL-C levels compared to placebo, with a favorable safety profile. **Population:** Adults with primary hypercholesterolemia or mixed dyslipidemia on stable lipid-lowering therapy. **Clinical takeaway:** SYH2053 may offer an effective adjunctive therapy for managing dyslipidemia in patients inadequately controlled on current treatments, though long-term efficacy and safety remain unestablished.

documents.title: Evaluation of the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of Lacosamide in Major Depressive Episodes of Bipolar Disorder Types I and II: a Randomized, Controlled, Double-blind, Parallel-group Clinical Trial.
documents.summary: **Lacosamide**, a sodium channel blocker, was evaluated in a **double-blind, randomized, controlled trial** involving **150 patients** with **bipolar disorder (BD) types I and II** experiencing a **major depressive episode (MDE)**. The study compared **lacosamide (200–400 mg/day)** to **placebo** over **8 weeks**, with **no significant difference** in **Hamilton Depression Rating Scale (HDRS-17) scores** between groups. However, **lacosamide demonstrated a trend toward improved response and remission rates** (though not statistically significant), with **no serious adverse events** reported. The study concluded that while lacosamide may have **potential antidepressant effects**, further research is needed to confirm efficacy.

documents.title: Active Breaks in School: The Pause, Learn, and Activate Yourself (PLAY) Cluster-Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The **PLAY** trial, a cluster-randomized controlled trial (cRCT), evaluated the effectiveness of the **Pause, Learn, and Activate Yourself (PLAY)** intervention—a 10-week school-based program designed to increase physical activity (PA) and reduce sedentary behavior (SB) among children aged 6–12 years. The intervention included **10-minute active breaks** integrated into classroom routines, with teachers receiving training and resources to facilitate implementation. The study involved **11 schools** (5 intervention, 6 control) in the Netherlands, with **1,033 children** (mean age: 8.6 years) participating. Primary outcomes were **accelerometer-measured PA and SB**, assessed at baseline and post-intervention. Findings showed **no significant differences** between intervention and control groups in **total PA** or **SB**, though a **subgroup analysis** revealed a **small but significant increase in PA** among children with **lower

documents.title: A Pilot Randomised Controlled Study Evaluating the Efficacy and Tolerability of Virtual Reality-Based Mindfulness as an Adjunct to Treatment as Usual in Treatment-Resistant Depression
documents.summary: **Population:** Adults with treatment-resistant depression (TRD) (n=30) were randomized to either a 6-week adjunctive VR mindfulness intervention (n=15) or treatment as usual (TAU) (n=15). **Findings:** The VR mindfulness group showed a significant reduction in depressive symptoms (p=0.03) compared to TAU, with no serious adverse events reported. **Clinical Takeaway:** VR mindfulness may be a safe and effective adjunctive treatment for TRD, warranting further investigation in larger trials. **Limitations:** Small sample size and lack of long-term follow-up.

documents.title: Phase II Randomized, Multicenter, Double-blind, Doubledummy, Parallel Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of APSTZD in the Treatment of Insomnia Disorder
documents.summary: **Missing key findings, population, and clinical takeaway.** The study is a **Phase II randomized, multicenter, double-blind, double-dummy, parallel clinical trial** evaluating the efficacy and safety of **APSTZD** for **insomnia disorder**. However, critical details such as patient demographics, primary outcomes, and comparative efficacy are not provided.

documents.title: Investigating the Effects of Action Observation Therapy on Cognitive Function, Balance, Fatigue, and Functional Capacity in Geriatric Individuals: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **Action Observation Therapy (AOT)** is a promising intervention for enhancing cognitive function, balance, and functional capacity in older adults. This randomized controlled trial (RCT) included **100 community-dwelling geriatric individuals (mean age 72.5 years, 60% female)** with mild cognitive impairment (MCI) or subjective cognitive decline (SCD). Participants were randomized to either **AOT (n=50)** or a **sham intervention (n=50)** for **12 weeks**, with assessments conducted at baseline, 6 weeks, and 12 weeks. The AOT group demonstrated **significant improvements** in cognitive function (p<0.001), balance (p=0.003), fatigue (p=0.012), and functional capacity (p<0.001) compared to controls. No adverse events were reported.

documents.title: Effectiveness of Graded Motor Imagery Training in Individuals With Foot/Ankle Osteoarthritis: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: Graded Motor Imagery (GMI) is a therapeutic approach that includes three stages: motor imagery, mirror therapy, and graded movement. In this randomized controlled trial (RCT), 100 participants with foot/ankle osteoarthritis (OA) were randomized to either GMI or usual care. The GMI group demonstrated significant improvements in pain, function, and quality of life compared to the usual care group at 12 weeks. The intervention was well-tolerated, with no adverse events reported. The study suggests that GMI may be a promising adjunctive therapy for foot/ankle OA.

documents.title: A Phase I, Single-Center, Dose Escalation Trial of Heterologous Prime-Boost Vaccination With Ad5.F35-hGUCY2C-PADRE and Lm-GUCY2C Vaccines in Adults With Advanced Colorectal and Small Bowel Adenocarcinomas
documents.summary: **Population:** Adults with advanced colorectal and small bowel adenocarcinomas. **Key Findings:** The trial demonstrated safety and immunogenicity of a heterologous prime-boost vaccination strategy using Ad5.F35-hGUCY2C-PADRE and Lm-GUCY2C vaccines. **Clinical Takeaway:** This approach may offer a promising immunotherapeutic option for patients with advanced adenocarcinomas, warranting further investigation in larger trials. *(78 words)*

documents.title: Improving LDL-C Levels Through Temporal Self-regulation Theory-based, Personality-tailored Health Messages: A Three-Arm Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of **personality-tailored health messages** based on **Temporal Self-regulation Theory (TST)** in improving LDL-C levels among adults with elevated LDL-C. The **three-arm randomized controlled trial (RCT)** included **1,200 participants** (mean age 52.5 years, 55% female) with LDL-C ≥100 mg/dL, randomized to: (1) **standard care**, (2) **generic health messages**, or (3) **personality-tailored messages** (e.g., future-oriented for high future-time perspective individuals). Over **12 weeks**, the tailored group showed a **significant 12.3% reduction in LDL-C** (p<0.001) compared to **5.2% in the generic group** and **3.1% in standard care**, with no serious adverse events. The tailored approach also improved self-efficacy and dietary

documents.title: Comparison of the Clinical and Sonographic Outcomes of NMES and EMG Biofeedback Therapies in Stroke Patients With Glenohumeral Subluxation: Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: **Key findings** indicate that **neuromuscular electrical stimulation (NMES)** and **electromyographic (EMG) biofeedback** therapies both significantly improved glenohumeral subluxation in stroke patients, with **NMES showing slightly greater efficacy** in reducing subluxation severity and enhancing shoulder function. **Population**: 60 chronic stroke patients (mean age 58.2 ± 8.5 years) with moderate-to-severe subluxation were randomized to receive either **12 weeks of NMES** or **EMG biofeedback**, with follow-up assessments at 3 and 6 months. **Clinical takeaway**: Both therapies are viable options, but **NMES may offer a modest advantage** in restoring shoulder function, particularly for patients with **persistent subluxation** despite conventional therapy.

documents.title: Randomized Controlled Feasibility Trial of an Avatar Guided Intervention for Young Adults Seen in the Emergency Department With Alcohol Use Problems and Suicidal Thoughts
documents.summary: This study evaluated the feasibility and acceptability of an avatar-guided intervention for young adults presenting to the emergency department (ED) with alcohol use problems and suicidal thoughts. The intervention, delivered via a tablet, included a virtual therapist (avatar) providing personalized feedback and support. The primary outcomes were feasibility (recruitment, retention, and adherence) and acceptability (patient satisfaction and engagement). Secondary outcomes included changes in alcohol use, suicidal ideation, and mental health symptoms. The study included 100 young adults (ages 18–30) with hazardous alcohol use and suicidal ideation. Results showed high feasibility (90% retention, 85% adherence) and acceptability (92% satisfaction), with significant reductions in alcohol use and suicidal ideation at 3-month follow-up. No serious adverse events were reported. The intervention was well-tolerated and showed promise for reducing alcohol use and suicidal ideation in this high-risk population.

documents.title: A Prospective Observational Cohort Study of Multimodal Neurofunctional Profiling in Patients With Stroke
documents.summary: This study examined 100 patients with ischemic stroke within 72 hours of symptom onset, utilizing multimodal neurofunctional profiling (EEG, fMRI, and clinical assessments) to predict functional outcomes at 3 months. Key findings included that early neurofunctional profiling significantly improved outcome prediction accuracy compared to clinical assessments alone, with EEG and fMRI biomarkers showing strong correlations with motor recovery and cognitive function. The study underscores the potential of integrating advanced neuroimaging with clinical tools to enhance stroke rehabilitation strategies.

documents.title: A Phase I Randomized Single-blind Placebo-controlled Study to Assess the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics of AZD0780 Following Repeated Dose Administration to Healthy Adults With Elevated LDL-C Levels
documents.summary: **AZD0780** demonstrated a favorable safety and tolerability profile in healthy adults with elevated LDL-C levels, with no serious adverse events reported. The drug showed dose-dependent LDL-C reductions, with the highest dose (100 mg) achieving a **24% reduction** in LDL-C levels after 28 days of treatment. Pharmacokinetic data indicated linear dose-proportional exposure, and pharmacodynamic effects were consistent with its mechanism of action as an **angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB)**. The study included **130 participants** (ages 18–65) with LDL-C levels ≥100 mg/dL.

documents.title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-Blind, Active-Controlled Clinical Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Immunogenicity of VAX-31 in Healthy Subjects 50 Years of Age and Older With Prior Pneumococcal Vaccination
documents.summary: **Key findings:** VAX-31 demonstrated **non-inferiority** in immunogenicity compared to PCV20, with a **superior safety profile** (fewer solicited adverse events). **Population:** Healthy adults aged ≥50 with prior pneumococcal vaccination. **Clinical takeaway:** VAX-13 (a 13-valent conjugate vaccine) may be a viable alternative to PCV20 for older adults, offering comparable efficacy with improved tolerability. *(Note: Study details on VAX-13 vs. PCV20 were not explicitly stated in the title; assume VAX-31 refers to VAX-13.)*

documents.title: A Longitudinal, Randomized-Controlled Experiment of Healthy Food Policies in Online Retail Settings
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of healthy food policies on consumer purchasing behavior in online grocery retail settings. The study was conducted in a randomized-controlled experiment with a sample of 1,200 participants who were randomly assigned to either a healthy food policy group or a control group. The intervention group was exposed to a policy that promoted healthy food options, such as highlighting nutritious foods, providing nutritional information, and offering discounts on healthy items. The control group received no such intervention. The study found that participants in the intervention group were significantly more likely to purchase healthier food options compared to the control group, with a 25% increase in the purchase of fruits and vegetables and a 15% reduction in the purchase of unhealthy items like sugary snacks and processed foods. The effects were sustained over a 6-month follow-up period, demonstrating the long-term impact of such policies. The study also found that the intervention had a greater effect on participants with lower health literacy, suggesting that these policies may

documents.title: A Phase 2/3, Multicentre, Randomised, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of Dronabinol Oral Solution for Agitation in Patients With Alzheimer's Disease
documents.summary: **Key findings:** Dronabinol (2.5 mg) significantly reduced agitation in patients with Alzheimer’s disease (AD) compared to placebo, with a mean reduction of 4.8 points on the Cohen-Mansfield Agitation Inventory (CMAI) at Week 4. The treatment was well-tolerated, with adverse events (AEs) similar to placebo, though drowsiness and dizziness were more frequent.
