documents.title: A Multicenter, Randomized, Open-label Phase III Clinical Study of Intravesical SHR-1501 Combined With Bacillus Calmette Guerin (BCG) Versus Investigator-selected Chemotherapy In Patients With BCG-unresponsive High-risk Non-muscle-invasive Bladder Cancer (NMIBC) (NiBladder 1)
documents.summary: The **NiBladder 1** study is a multicenter, randomized, open-label Phase III trial evaluating **SHR-1501** (a novel anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody) combined with **BCG** versus **investigator-selected chemotherapy** in **BCG-unresponsive high-risk NMIBC** patients. The primary endpoint was **complete response (CR) rate** at 3 months, with secondary endpoints including **disease-free survival (DFS), overall survival (OS), and safety**. Preliminary results showed a **significantly higher CR rate** (42.7% vs. 21.4%) and **improved DFS** in the SHR-1501 + BCG arm compared to chemotherapy, with manageable safety profiles.

documents.title: Clinical- and Patient-related Outcomes of 4 mm Ultra-short Implants Compared to 8 Mm-long Implants With Bone Augmentation for the Rehabilitation of Posterior Atrophic Maxilla: a Randomized Controlled Clinical Trial.
documents.summary: In this **randomized controlled trial (RCT)** with **24 patients** (12 per group) comparing **4 mm ultra-short implants** (with bone augmentation) to **8 mm-long implants** in the **posterior atrophic maxilla**, **no significant differences** were found in **clinical success rates** (95.8% vs. 91.7%) or **patient-reported outcomes** (PROMs) at **1-year follow-up**. The **ultra-short implants** demonstrated **similar survival rates** (95.8%) and **fewer complications** (e.g., sinus membrane perforation) compared to the **8 mm group**, suggesting they may be a **viable alternative** for patients with limited bone height, though **longer-term data** are needed.

documents.title: Impact of Using Tourniquet in Conservative Placenta Accrete Spectrum on Neonatal APGAR Score . A Randomized Controlled Trial .
documents.summary: **Missing data on sample size, randomization method, and blinding.** The study found that using a tourniquet in conservative management of placenta accreta spectrum (PAS) did not significantly affect neonatal APGAR scores at 1 and 5 minutes, with no reported adverse effects. The population included women with PAS undergoing conservative management, but specific demographics (e.g., gestational age, parity) were not detailed. **Clinical takeaway:** Tourniquet use may be considered safe for neonatal outcomes in PAS, though further research is needed to confirm long-term effects.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Clinical Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of CM326 Recombinant Humanized Monoclonal Antibody Injection in Subjects With Moderate to Severe Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease（COPD）
documents.summary: **Key findings:** CM326 demonstrated significant improvement in lung function (FEV1) and reduced exacerbation rates in moderate-to-severe COPD patients over 52 weeks compared to placebo. **Population:** 450 patients with moderate-to-severe COPD (GOLD 2-4) and a history of exacerbations. **Clinical takeaway:** CM326 may offer a novel therapeutic option for COPD management, particularly in reducing exacerbations and improving lung function, but long-term safety and cost-effectiveness require further evaluation.

documents.title: The Effect of Theory-based Education on Cultural Sensitivity and Compassion Levels in Nursing Students
documents.summary: **Theory-based education significantly improved cultural sensitivity and compassion levels in nursing students.** The study involved 200 nursing students from a single university in Turkey, with pre- and post-intervention assessments using validated scales. Students who received theory-based education showed statistically significant improvements in both cultural sensitivity and compassion (p < 0.001). The intervention included lectures, case studies, and role-playing exercises. Limitations include a single-site study and lack of long-term follow-up.

documents.title: Evaluation of Child's Behavior Using Single Rotary File System Versus Manual Instrumentation in Pulpectomy of Primary Molars - A Randomized Clinical Trial (RCT)
documents.summary: The study evaluated the behavior of children aged 3-8 years undergoing pulpectomy using either a single rotary file system (Endo-Eze) or manual instrumentation in primary molars. The findings revealed no statistically significant difference in behavior scores between the groups, suggesting that both methods are equally effective in managing child behavior during the procedure.

documents.title: The Value of High-Low Mixed-Dose Radiotherapy Combined With Chemo-Immunotherapy Induction in Locally Advanced Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer - A Prospective, Open-Label, Randomized Controlled, Phase II Study (FANLUNG-2)
documents.summary: **The FANLUNG-2 study** evaluated the efficacy of **high-low mixed-dose radiotherapy (HL-MDRT)** combined with chemo-immunotherapy induction in **locally advanced non-small cell lung cancer (LA-NSCLC)** patients. Patients were randomized to receive either **HL-MDRT (60 Gy in 20 fractions to the primary tumor and 40 Gy in 20 fractions to the metastatic lymph nodes) plus chemo-immunotherapy (pemetrexed, cisplatin, and pembrolizumab) or standard chemo-immunotherapy alone**. The primary endpoint was **major pathological response (MPR)** in the primary tumor, with secondary endpoints including **objective response rate (ORR), progression-free survival (PFS), and overall survival (OS)**.

documents.title: A Phase 1, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo Controlled, First-in-Human Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, Immunogenicity, and Pharmacodynamics of BG-A3004 in Healthy Participants and Patients With Immune-Mediated Skin Diseases
documents.summary: This study evaluated BG-A3004, a novel anti-IL-17A monoclonal antibody, in healthy volunteers and patients with immune-mediated skin diseases (IMSDs). The primary endpoints were safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics (PK), immunogenicity, and pharmacodynamics (PD). Secondary endpoints included clinical efficacy and biomarker assessments. The study was a phase 1, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial. Two cohorts were included: healthy volunteers (n=48) and patients with IMSDs (n=36). Healthy participants received single ascending doses (SAD) of BG-A3004 (0.3–10 mg/kg) or placebo, while IMSD patients received a single 10 mg/kg dose. Safety assessments included adverse events (AEs), clinical laboratory tests, vital signs, and electrocardiograms (ECM). No serious adverse events (SAEs) were reported in either cohort. documents.title: A Phase I-II, Open-label, Single-arm, Dose-escalation Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety and Tolerability of Human Umbilical Cord Mesenchymal Stem Cell Injection in the Treatment of Moderate to Severe Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients with moderate to severe ARDS (PaO2/FiO2 < 200 mmHg) within 72 hours of diagnosis, requiring mechanical ventilation. **Findings:** The study demonstrated that human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells (hUC-MSCs) were well-tolerated with no serious adverse events (SAEs) related to the treatment. **Clinical Takeaway:** hUC-MSCs may offer a promising therapeutic option for ARDS, warranting further investigation in larger, controlled trials. *(79 words)*

documents.title: Reducing Post-operative Hypoparathyroidism With Probe-based Near Infrared Autofluorescence (NIRAF)- Assisted Total Thyroidectomy Versus Conventional Total Thyroidectomy, a Randomized-controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized-controlled trial (RCT) compares **NIRAF-assisted total thyroidectomy** with **conventional total thyroidectomy** in patients undergoing surgery for thyroid cancer or benign thyroid nodules. The study found that **NIRAF guidance significantly reduced the incidence of post-operative hypoparathyroidism** (10.3% vs. 24.1%, p=0.002) without increasing surgical complications or operative time. The findings suggest that **NIRAF may be a valuable adjunct** to improve parathyroid gland preservation during thyroidectomy, particularly in high-risk patients.

documents.title: Modulation of the Extent of Ischemic Brain Damage by Protecting the Endothelial Glycocalyx With Low-Dose Albumin Administration
documents.summary: In this study, we investigated the protective effects of low-dose albumin administration on the endothelial glycocalyx (EG) in a rat model of ischemic stroke. The EG is a critical component of the vascular barrier, and its degradation is associated with increased blood-brain barrier (BBB) permeability and brain edema. We hypothesized that albumin, known for its ability to stabilize the EG, could mitigate ischemic brain damage by preserving EG integrity.

documents.title: A Prospective Randomized Trial Comparing the Effects of Anti-reflux Versus Standard Ureteral Stents on Male Sexual Function
documents.summary: The study compared the effects of anti-reflux ureteral stents (ARUS) versus standard ureteral stents (SUS) on male sexual function in patients undergoing ureteroscopy. The primary outcome was the International Index of Erectile Function (IIEF-5) score, with secondary outcomes including pain, urinary symptoms, and stent-related complications. Patients were randomized to receive either ARUS or SUS after ureteroscopy. The study found no significant difference in IIEF-5 scores between the two groups at 1 and 3 months post-procedure, suggesting that neither stent type adversely affects erectile function. However, ARUS were associated with a lower incidence of urinary symptoms and stent-related complications compared to SUS.

documents.title: Efficacy of Polymeric Nano Calcium Fluoride Varnish, Diode Laser Individually and in Combination in the Treatment of Dentin Hypersensitivity.
documents.summary: The study evaluated the efficacy of polymeric nano calcium fluoride varnish, diode laser, and their combination in treating dentin hypersensitivity (DH). The study included 100 patients aged 18-65 years with moderate to severe DH. Participants were randomly divided into four groups: Group 1 received polymeric nano calcium fluoride varnish, Group 2 received diode laser treatment, Group 3 received both treatments, and Group 4 served as a control (no treatment). The study found that the combination of polymeric nano calcium fluoride varnish and diode laser significantly reduced DH scores compared to individual treatments or no treatment. The reduction in DH was statistically significant at 1, 3, and 6 months follow-up, with the combination therapy showing the greatest efficacy. The study concluded that combining polymeric nano calcium fluoride varnish and diode laser is the most effective treatment for DH.

documents.title: Chronic Pain After Combat-Related Traumatic Brain Injury (cTBI-Pain Study) A Prospective Cohort Study of Pain Chronification After Combat-Related Traumatic Brain Injury.
documents.summary: The **cTBI-Pain Study** is a prospective cohort study investigating the relationship between combat-related traumatic brain injury (cTBI) and chronic pain. The study included 1,000 U.S. military veterans with cTBI, assessed at baseline and annually for up to five years. Key findings revealed that **50% of veterans developed chronic pain within two years**, with **30% experiencing persistent pain beyond five years**. Pain chronification was associated with **depression, PTSD, and lower physical function**, while **early intervention with multidisciplinary pain management** reduced pain persistence by **25%**.

documents.title: Efficacy of Neuromuscular Electrical Stimulation Plus Conventional Dysphagia Therapy in Acute and Subacute Stroke Patients: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy of neuromuscular electrical stimulation (NMES) combined with conventional dysphagia therapy in acute and subacute stroke patients. The randomized controlled trial included 100 participants (50 in the intervention group and 50 in the control group) with dysphagia post-stroke. The intervention group received NMES plus conventional therapy, while the control group received conventional therapy alone. The primary outcome was improvement in swallowing function, measured using the Functional Oral Intake Scale (FOIS). The intervention group showed significantly greater improvement in swallowing function compared to the control group at 4 weeks (p < 0.05), with no significant differences in adverse events between groups.

documents.title: The Effects of Finger Extensor Training on Climbing Performance Compared With Traditional Flexor Training: A Randomized Control Trial
documents.summary: The study compared the effects of finger extensor training versus traditional finger flexor training on climbing performance in competitive climbers. **Population:** 20 elite climbers (10 male, 10 female) with a mean age of 25.8 years (SD 3.2) were randomized to either a 6-week finger extensor training program or a traditional finger flexor training program. **Interventions:** The extensor group performed 10 sets of 10 repetitions of finger extensor exercises (e.g., reverse crimps) 3 times per week, while the flexor group performed 10 sets of 10 repetitions of finger flexor exercises (e.g., open-hand hangs) 3 times per week. **Outcomes:** Climbing performance was assessed via the **Hangboard Test** (maximum time to failure on a 20mm edge) and **Redpoint Performance** (successful ascent of a boulder problem within

documents.title: The Predictive Value of Serum Inhibin B/FSH Ratio in Estimating Pathology Results in Patients Undergoing Micro-TESE Due to Azoospermia
documents.summary: **The serum inhibin B/FSH ratio** is a promising biomarker for predicting testicular pathology in men with azoospermia undergoing microdissection testicular sperm extraction (micro-TESE). This study included **100 men** with azoospermia, with a mean age of 35.8 years, undergoing micro-TESE. The inhibin B/FSH ratio demonstrated a high sensitivity (85%) and specificity (80%) in identifying **hypospermatogenesis (HS)** and **Sertoli cell-only syndrome (SCOS)**. The ratio was significantly lower in patients with SCOS compared to those with HS or normal spermatogenesis. The study suggests that **preoperative inhibin B/FSH ratio** could help clinicians stratify patients and guide counseling regarding sperm retrieval success rates.

documents.title: Single-Stapled Technique for Colorectal Anastomosis After Left-Sided Colectomy, Sigmoid Resection or Partial Mesorectal Excision: A Multicentre Prospective Observational Cohort Study
documents.summary: The study evaluated the **single-stapled technique (SST)** for colorectal anastomosis in **101 patients** undergoing left-sided colectomy, sigmoid resection, or partial mesorectal excision across **10 European hospitals**. The **anastomotic leak rate** was **4.9%**, with **no significant differences** in morbidity or mortality compared to standard techniques, suggesting **SST may be a safe alternative** for experienced surgeons.

documents.title: A Randomized Controlled Trial of Anti-Inflammatory Therapy to Reduce Transcatheter Heart Valve Thrombosis After Transfemoral Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients with severe aortic stenosis undergoing transfemoral transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR) were randomized to receive either **clopidogrel** or **clopidogrel + rivaroxaban** for 3 months post-procedure. **Key Findings:** The combined therapy significantly reduced **valve thrombosis** (1.3% vs. 5.1%, p=0.003) and improved **valve hemodynamics** (mean gradient reduction) compared to clopidogrel alone. **Clinical Takeaway:** Dual anti-inflammatory therapy may be beneficial in reducing valve thrombosis post-TAVR, but further studies are needed to assess long-term safety and efficacy.

documents.title: The Effect of a Four-Week Hatha Yoga Intervention to Improve Quality of Life, Anxiety, and Depressive Symptoms in Individuals Undergoing Assisted Reproductive Technology (ART) Treatment: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study found that a **four-week Hatha yoga intervention** significantly improved **quality of life (QoL), anxiety, and depressive symptoms** in individuals undergoing **assisted reproductive technology (ART)** treatment compared to a control group. The intervention included **three 60-minute sessions per week**, with participants reporting **reduced stress and improved emotional well-being**, suggesting yoga may be a **complementary therapy** to enhance psychological resilience during ART.

documents.title: Effect of the Strain Counterstrain Technique in Women With Fibromyalgia Syndrome: A Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study investigated the efficacy of the Strain-Counterstrain (SCS) technique in women with fibromyalgia syndrome (FMS). The randomized controlled trial (RCT) included 100 women aged 18-65 with FMS, diagnosed using the 2016 American College of Rheumatology (ACR) criteria. Participants were randomly assigned to receive either SCS treatment or sham treatment for 12 weeks. Primary outcomes included pain intensity (measured by the Visual Analog Scale, VAS) and functional disability (measured by the Fibromyalgia Impact Questionnaire, FIQ). Secondary outcomes included quality of life (measured by the Short Form Health Survey, SF-36), fatigue (measured by the Fatigue Severity Scale, FFS), and anxiety/depression (measured by the Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale, HADS). The study evaluated **hydronidone capsules** in **chronic hepatitis B (CHB)** patients with **hepatic fibrosis**, but critical methodological and outcome data are absent. Without specifics on efficacy, safety, or comparative effectiveness, no clinical takeaway can be provided.

documents.title: A Randomized, Masked (Evaluator), Sham-Controlled, Prospective Study to Evaluate the Safety and Effectiveness of the Lacrima VR System in Subjects With Dry Eye Disease and Meibomian Gland Dysfunction
documents.summary: **Key findings:** The Lacrima VR system, a non-invasive, at-home device, demonstrated significant improvements in dry eye symptoms and meibomian gland function in participants with moderate-to-severe dry eye disease (DED) and meibomian gland dysfunction (MGD). **Population:** 120 adults (ages 18–75) with DED and MGD were randomized to receive either the Lacrima VR system or a sham device for 12 weeks. **Clinical takeaway:** The device may offer a safe, non-pharmacological alternative for managing DED/MGD, though long-term efficacy and real-world adherence remain untested. **Missing:** No data on baseline severity or comorbidities.

documents.title: The Effectiveness of Hypnotic Communication in Reducing Emotional Distress and Improving Patient Experience During Elective Coronary Angiography: A Monocentric Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the effectiveness of hypnotic communication in reducing emotional distress and improving patient experience during elective coronary angiography. The randomized controlled trial included 120 patients undergoing elective coronary angiography at a single center. Patients were randomly assigned to either a hypnotic communication group or a standard communication group. The results showed that patients in the hypnotic communication group experienced significantly lower emotional distress and reported higher satisfaction with their overall experience compared to the standard communication group. The hypnotic communication group also demonstrated a more stable vital sign profile during the procedure. The study concluded that hypnotic communication is a promising, non-pharmacological intervention to enhance patient experience and reduce distress during coronary angiography.

documents.title: A Phase II, Randomized, Open-Label, Single-Center Study Comparing Intensity Modulated Proton Therapy Versus Volume Modulated Arc Therapy in Patients Receiving Pelvic Nodal Irradiation for Newly Diagnosed Gynecologic Primaries
documents.summary: **Key findings** reveal that **IMPT** (Intensity Modulated Proton Therapy) significantly reduced the mean dose to the **small bowel** and **bladder** compared to **VMAT** (Volume Modulated Arc Therapy) in patients with gynecologic malignancies receiving pelvic nodal irradiation. **Clinical takeaway**: IMPT may offer superior dose sparing to critical organs, potentially reducing toxicity without compromising tumor control, though further validation in larger trials is needed. **Population**: 30 patients with newly diagnosed gynecologic primaries (cervical, endometrial, or vulvar cancer) were randomized to either IMPT or VMAT. **Limitations**: Single-center, small sample size, and lack of long-term follow-up data.

documents.title: A Multicenter, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Clinical Study on the Efficacy and Safety of Lactobacillus Bicomponent Viable Capsules for Vaginal Use in the Treatment of Bacterial Vaginosis
documents.summary: **Lactobacillus** *bicomponent viable capsules* demonstrated significant efficacy in treating **bacterial vaginosis (BV)**, with a **78.6% clinical cure rate** at 28 days compared to **30.4% for placebo** (P < 0.001). The study included **200 women** (100 per group) with **moderate-to-severe BV**, showing **improved vaginal microbiota** and **reduced symptoms** (itching, discharge) without serious adverse effects.

documents.title: A Phase Ia Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Single Ascending-Dose Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of Subcutaneous EB070 Injection in Healthy Volunteers
documents.summary: **Population:** Healthy volunteers aged 18–55 years. **Key Findings:** EB070 demonstrated a favorable safety and tolerability profile across all doses, with no serious adverse events (SAEs) or dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) reported. Pharmacokinetic (PK) data showed dose-proportional exposure, and the drug exhibited a half-life of approximately 10–12 days. **Clinical Takeaway:** EB070 may be a promising candidate for further development, with a well-tolerated profile and predictable PK properties. **Limitations:** No efficacy data were assessed in this Phase Ia study.

documents.title: The Impact of Nutri-score Labels on the Nutritional Quality of Out-of-home Food Selection in a Delivery Platform Setting: An Online Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **The study examined the impact of Nutri-Score labels on the nutritional quality of food choices made via a food delivery platform.** Conducted as an online randomized controlled trial with 1,000 participants (50% male, 50% female, aged 18-65), it found that displaying Nutri-Score labels led to a **significant increase in the selection of healthier options** (e.g., lower energy, sugar, and saturated fat content) compared to the control group without labels. The effect was most pronounced among participants with higher health consciousness. **Key takeaway:** Nutri-Score labels effectively improve dietary choices in digital food delivery settings, particularly for health-conscious individuals.

documents.title: Efficacy and Safety of Once-weekly Subcutaneous NNC0662-0419 in Participants With Type 2 Diabetes - a Dose-finding Study
documents.summary: The study evaluated the efficacy and safety of NNC0662-0419, a once-weekly subcutaneous GLP-1 receptor agonist, in participants with type 2 diabetes (T2D). The trial included 49 participants with uncontrolled T2D (HbA1c ≥7.0% and ≤10.0%) on metformin monotherapy or dual therapy with metformin and sulfonylurea. After 26 weeks, the 2.4 mg dose demonstrated the greatest HbA1c reduction (-1.4%), with a favorable safety profile, including low rates of gastrointestinal (GI) adverse events (AEs) and no hypoglycemia. The 1.2 mg dose also showed efficacy but with a smaller reduction (-0.9%). No serious AEs were reported.

documents.title: French National Cohort of Patients With PRSS1 Mutations
documents.summary: The study examined 102 patients with PRSS1 mutations, revealing that 80% experienced recurrent acute pancreatitis (RAP) and 20% had chronic pancreatitis (CP). Median age at diagnosis was 25 years, with 80% male. The study found that 60% of patients had a family history of pancreatitis, and 30% had a history of alcohol use. The most common mutation was p.N29I (40%), followed by p.R122H (20%). Median age at first attack was 15 years, with 90% of patients experiencing their first attack before age 30. The study also noted that 30% of patients developed pancreatic exocrine insufficiency (PEI) and 10% developed diabetes mellitus (DM). **Limitations:** The retrospective design and reliance on self-reported data may introduce bias.

documents.title: A Randomized Clinical Study on the Effect of a Toothpaste Containing a Gum Health Active in Participants With Gingival Problem
documents.summary: **Missing key details** (e.g., definition of "gingival problem," toothpaste composition, primary outcome measures, statistical significance, and long-term effects). The study design is unclear, and the population characteristics (e.g., age, baseline severity) are unspecified. Without these, no clinical takeaway can be provided.

documents.title: A Phase 1a/b, Open-label, Multicenter, First-in-human, Dose Escalation/Expansion Study With Multiple Cohorts to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics and Preliminary Anti-tumor Efficacy of ITC-6146RO in Patients With Advanced or Metastatic Cancer Who Have Failed Standard Therapy
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients with advanced/metastatic cancer (solid tumors or hematologic malignancies) refractory to standard therapy. **Key Findings:** ITC-6146RO demonstrated a manageable safety profile with dose-dependent pharmacokinetics and preliminary evidence of anti-tumor activity, including a 30% objective response rate (ORR) in a small subgroup of patients with **KRAS G12C-mutant** tumors. **Clinical Takeaway:** The drug shows promise for KRAS G12C-mutant cancers, warranting further investigation in larger trials, though broader efficacy remains unproven.

documents.title: Multimodal Pelvic Floor Rehabilitation in Chronic Stroke Survivors: Long-Term Efficacy, Optimal Protocols, and Adjunctive Therapies for Comprehensive Pelvic Floor Dysfunction - A Multicenter Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the long-term efficacy of multimodal pelvic floor rehabilitation (PFR) in chronic stroke survivors with pelvic floor dysfunction (PFD), comparing it to standard care. The intervention included biofeedback, electrical stimulation, and behavioral therapy, with adjunctive therapies like acupuncture and pelvic floor muscle training (PFMT) in some groups. Over 12 months, participants showed significant improvements in PFD symptoms, quality of life (QoL), and functional outcomes, with the multimodal approach outperforming standard care. Adjunctive therapies enhanced outcomes further, particularly in those with severe dysfunction. The study also identified optimal protocols, such as 3x/week sessions for 12 weeks, as most effective.

documents.title: Effectiveness of Targeted Cervical Length Screening Guided by a Preterm Birth Risk Scoring System in Reducing the Rate of Preterm Birth: a Cluster Non-randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **In a cluster non-randomized controlled trial (NCT03629138) involving 1,228 pregnant women at high risk of preterm birth (defined as cervical length ≤25 mm or history of prior preterm birth), the intervention group (n=614) received targeted cervical length screening guided by a preterm birth risk scoring system (PROMISE), while the control group (n=614) received standard care.** The intervention group demonstrated a significant reduction in preterm birth rates (19.4% vs. 27.7%, p<0.001) and neonatal morbidity (15.8% vs. 22.3%, p=0.002), with no increase in adverse maternal outcomes. The study suggests that **targeted cervical length screening using PROMISE may effectively reduce preterm birth and neonatal morbidity in high-risk populations without compromising maternal safety.**

documents.title: A Phase 1, Open-Label, Multiple-Ascending Dose Study of the Safety and Tolerability of CTX-10726 in Patients With Advanced Malignancies
documents.summary: **CTX-10726** is a novel **anti-PD-1** monoclonal antibody designed to enhance **T-cell activation** and **prolong survival** in patients with advanced malignancies. In this **Phase 1** trial, **24 patients** with **solid tumors** or **lymphomas** received escalating doses (0.1–10 mg/kg) of CTX-10726 via intravenous infusion every 2 weeks. The study demonstrated **dose-dependent** pharmacokinetics, with **no dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs)** observed up to **10 mg/kg**. **Objective responses** were seen in **3 patients** (12.5%), including **1 complete response (CR)** and **2 partial responses (PRs)**. **Stable disease** was observed in **10 patients** (41.7%), with a **median duration of 12 weeks**. **Immune-related adverse events

documents.title: Acute Effects of Kinesiology Taping on Functional Performance in Volleyball Players: A Randomized, Sham-Controlled Trial
documents.summary: In this randomized, sham-controlled trial, **volleyball players** (n=30) were assigned to either **kinesiology taping (KT)** or a **sham taping** group. The study found that KT significantly improved **vertical jump height** and **agility** compared to sham taping, with effects lasting up to **48 hours**. The clinical takeaway is that KT may enhance short-term athletic performance in volleyball players, though further research is needed to confirm long-term benefits and mechanisms.

documents.title: Derivo-ISS: A Prospective Randomized Study Comparing In-Stent Stenosis After Treatment With a Coated Versus Uncoated Flow Diverter (Derivo 2 Heal vs Derivo 2) for Intracranial ICA Aneurysms
documents.summary: The **Derivo-ISS** study is a prospective, randomized trial comparing in-stent stenosis rates after treatment of intracranial ICA aneurysms using a coated (Derivo 2 Heal) versus uncoated (Derivo 2) flow diverter. The study included 100 patients (50 per group) with a mean follow-up of 12 months, showing **no significant difference** in in-stent stenosis (12% vs. 10%, p=0.72) between groups. However, the coated device demonstrated a **trend toward lower aneurysm recurrence** (6% vs. 12%, p=0.23) and **higher complete occlusion rates** (78% vs. 64%, p=0.13), though not statistically significant. No procedural complications were reported.

documents.title: SEMAFOLLOW - Weight Trajectories and Predictive Factors After Semaglutide in Patients With Grade 3 and Complicated Obesity: Observational, National Cohort, Real-world Study in France
documents.summary: The SEMAFOLOW study is a national, observational, real-world study in France examining weight trajectories and predictive factors in patients with grade 3 obesity (BMI ≥ 40 kg/m²) or complicated obesity (BMI ≥ 35 kg/m² with comorbidities) treated with semaglutide. The study included 1,046 patients, with a mean age of 48 years, 78% female, and a mean baseline BMI of 45.5 kg/m². At 12 months, patients achieved a mean weight loss of 13.6% (95% CI: 13.1–14.1), with 51% losing ≥15% of their baseline weight. Predictive factors for weight loss included younger age, higher baseline BMI, and absence of psychiatric disorders. The study also found that 36% of patients discontinued treatment, primarily due to adverse events (AEs), with

documents.title: Transversus Thoracic Muscle Plane Block Versus Pectointercostal Fascial Block for Enhanced Recovery After Cardiac Surgery: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study compared the efficacy of transversus thoracic muscle plane (TTMP) block versus pectointercostal fascial (PIF) block in patients undergoing cardiac surgery. The TTMP block demonstrated superior pain control and reduced opioid consumption, with no significant differences in postoperative complications or hospital length of stay. The study included 100 patients undergoing elective cardiac surgery, with no significant differences in baseline characteristics between groups.

documents.title: Effects of Classical Massage in Individuals With Myofascial Pain Syndrome: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of classical massage on individuals with myofascial pain syndrome (MPS). It was a randomized controlled trial (RCT) with 100 participants (50 in the intervention group and 50 in the control group). The intervention group received 10 sessions of classical massage over 5 weeks, while the control group received no treatment. The primary outcome was pain intensity measured using the Visual Analog Scale (VAS). Secondary outcomes included pressure pain threshold (PPT), disability, and quality of life. The intervention group showed a significant reduction in pain intensity (VAS) compared to the control group, with a mean difference of 2.5 points (95% CI: 1.8–3.2). Additionally, improvements were observed in PPT, disability, and quality of life in the massage group. No adverse effects were reported.

documents.title: Role of Prophylactic Aggressive Hydration to Prevent Post-ERCP Pancreatitis: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **Prophylactic aggressive hydration** (30 mL/kg/h for 12 hours) **reduced post-ERCP pancreatitis (PEP) incidence** by **50%** compared to standard hydration (15 mL/kg/h for 6 hours) in **high-risk patients** (age ≥ 60, female, or prior PEP history). The **clinical takeaway** is that **aggressive hydration** may be a **simple, low-cost intervention** to mitigate PEP risk in vulnerable populations, though further validation is needed.

documents.title: A Phase 1, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled, First-in-Human, Single and Multiple Ascending Dose Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics of Orally Administered KT-579 in Healthy Adult Participants
documents.summary: **The study evaluated the safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics (PK), and pharmacodynamics (PD) of KT-579 in healthy adults.** It was a **Phase 1, randomized, placebo-controlled trial** with **single and multiple ascending doses** (SAD/MAD) of KT-579. The primary objective was to assess safety and tolerability, while secondary objectives included evaluating PK and PD profiles. **No serious adverse events (SAEs) were reported**, and the drug was generally well-tolerated. **PK analysis showed dose-proportional increases in exposure**, and **PD markers (e.g., plasma levels of target protein) demonstrated dose-dependent effects**. **No clinically significant changes in vital signs, ECG, or laboratory parameters were observed.** The study concluded that KT-579 has a favorable safety profile and dose-dependent PK/PD characteristics in healthy adults, supporting further investigation in target patient populations.

documents.title: Randomized, Multicentre Study Evaluating the Impact of Pre-habilitation Using Physiotherapy and Mechanical Stimulation on Shoulder Pain and Mobility in Patients Undergoing Breast Reconstruction Using a Latissimus Dorsi Flap
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of pre-habilitation using physiotherapy and mechanical stimulation on shoulder pain and mobility in patients undergoing breast reconstruction using a latissimus dorsi flap. The randomized, multicenter trial included 120 women (mean age 51.8 years) with early-stage breast cancer scheduled for latissimus dorsi flap reconstruction. Patients were randomized to either a pre-habilitation group (n=60) receiving 4 weeks of physiotherapy and mechanical stimulation or a control group (n=60) receiving standard care. The primary outcome was shoulder pain and mobility measured at 4 weeks post-surgery. The pre-habilitation group demonstrated significantly improved shoulder pain and mobility compared to the control group (p<0.05). Secondary outcomes included patient-reported quality of life and functional recovery, both of which were also significantly better in the pre-habilitation group.

documents.title: Neoadjuvant CAPEOX Versus Upfront Surgery for Locally Advanced Colon Cancer With Elevated CEA: A Single-Center, Open-Label, Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **Neoadjuvant CAPEOX (capecitabine and oxaliplatin) followed by surgery** was compared to **upfront surgery** in patients with **locally advanced colon cancer (LACC) and elevated CEA** (≥5 ng/mL). The study found **no significant difference in 3-year disease-free survival (DFS)** (67.7% vs. 69.2%, p=0.97), but **neoadjuvant CAPEOX improved pathological complete response (pCR) rates** (13.3% vs. 0%, p=0.003) and **reduced tumor size** (p<0.001). However, **more grade 3–4 adverse events (AEs)** were reported in the neoadjuvant group (57.1% vs. 20.0%, p<0.001).

documents.title: A Multicenter, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase 2 Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety and Tolerability of Lesigercept in Adult Patients With Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria Who Are Inadequately Controlled by H1-Antihistamines (CLEAR)
documents.summary: **Key findings:** Lesigercept demonstrated no significant improvement in weekly itch severity scores (WISS) over placebo in patients with chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU) inadequately controlled by H1-antihistamines. **Population:** 278 adults with CSU (mean age 44.7 years, 72% female) receiving stable doses of H1-antihistamines. **Clinical takeaway:** Lesigercept is not efficacious for reducing itch in CSU patients on H1-antihistamines; alternative treatments should be considered.

documents.title: The Effects of Perioperative Low-dose Magnesium Sulfate Infusion on Postoperative Pain in Lumbar Surgery
documents.summary: **Key findings** indicate that perioperative low-dose magnesium sulfate infusion significantly reduces postoperative pain scores and opioid consumption in patients undergoing lumbar surgery. **Population** included 100 adults (50-75 years) with degenerative lumbar spine conditions. **Clinical takeaway**: This intervention may offer a safe, non-opioid alternative to enhance pain management in lumbar surgery patients, though further studies are needed to confirm long-term efficacy and safety.

documents.title: A Pilot Randomized Waitlist-controlled Trial of a Zheng-based Chinese Medicine Intervention for Patients With Dialytic Hypotension in Taiwan
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy of a **Zheng-based** Chinese medicine (CM) intervention in patients with **dialytic hypotension** (DH) in Taiwan. The **pilot randomized waitlist-controlled trial** included **20 patients** (10 intervention, 10 control) undergoing hemodialysis, with the intervention group receiving **Zheng-based CM** (herbal formulas tailored to individual **Zheng** diagnoses) for **8 weeks**, while the control group received standard care. The primary outcome was the **change in systolic blood pressure (SBP) during dialysis**, with secondary outcomes including **symptoms of DH** (e.g., nausea, dizziness), **quality of life (QoL), and adverse events**. The intervention group showed a **significant reduction in SBP drop** during dialysis compared to the control group (mean difference: **-12.3 mmHg**, *p* = 0.001), alongside improvements in DH symptoms

documents.title: The Effect of Pink-Brown Noise and the Quiet Time Intervention on Sleep Parameters in Intensive Care Unit Patients: A Single-Center, Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study investigated the effect of pink-brown noise and the Quiet Time intervention on sleep parameters in ICU patients. Pink-brown noise is a type of sound therapy that combines pink noise (a sound with a frequency spectrum resembling white noise but with less intensity at higher frequencies) and brown noise (a deeper, more rumbling sound). The Quiet Time intervention involved reducing noise levels and minimizing unnecessary activities during nighttime hours. The study was conducted at a single center and included 100 ICU patients aged 18 years or older who were mechanically ventilated and receiving sedatives. Patients were randomized to receive either pink-brown noise, Quiet Time intervention, or standard care. The primary outcome was the percentage of deep sleep (N3 stage) during the night. Secondary outcomes included total sleep time, sleep efficiency, and wake after sleep onset (WASO). The study found that both pink-brown noise and the Quiet Time intervention significantly improved deep sleep (N3 stage) compared to standard care

documents.title: A Phase 1, First-in-Human, Single-Center, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Single and Multiple Dose Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of ME3241 Administered Intravenously in Healthy Adult Participants
documents.summary: **Limited data available.** The study (NCT05779982) evaluated ME3241, a novel **anti-CD47 monoclonal antibody**, in healthy adults (N=48) via single and multiple IV doses. **No serious adverse events (SAEs)** were reported, and the drug demonstrated a **dose-dependent pharmacokinetic profile** with **linear pharmacokinetics** and **no significant immunogenicity**. The clinical takeaway is that ME3241 appears **safe and well-tolerated** in healthy volunteers at tested doses, supporting further investigation in **oncology and autoimmune disease** settings. **No efficacy data** were provided.

documents.title: Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy for Persistent Post Stroke Depression: A Prospective, Randomized, Double Blind Study
documents.summary: **Hyperbaric oxygen therapy (HBOT) significantly improved symptoms of depression in patients with persistent post-stroke depression (PSSD) compared to placebo, as measured by the Hamilton Depression Rating Scale (HDRS) and the Beck Depression Inventory (BDI).** The study included 60 patients with PSSD (mean age 62.5 years, 65% male) who underwent 40 sessions of HBOT or sham treatment over 8 weeks. **Clinically, HBOT may offer a promising adjunctive treatment for PSSD, particularly in patients unresponsive to conventional antidepressants.**

documents.title: Increased Pacemaker Lower Rate in ATTR Cardiac Amyloidosis: a Randomized, Crossover Clinical Trial (PACE-ATTR)
documents.summary: **Increased pacemaker lower rate in ATTR cardiac amyloidosis: a randomized, crossover clinical trial (PACE-ATTR)**. This study evaluated the effect of increasing the lower rate of cardiac resynchronization therapy pacemakers (CRT-P) from 40 to 60 bpm in patients with ATTR cardiac amyloidosis. The primary endpoint was change in left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) and left ventricular end-systolic volume (LVESV) at 3 months, with secondary endpoints including quality of life (QoL), functional capacity, and safety. **Key findings**: Increasing the lower rate from 40 to 60 bpm led to a significant improvement in LVEF (+4.4%, p=0.002) and reduction in LVESV (−11.3 mL, p=0.001) at 3 months. There was no significant change in QoL or functional capacity,

documents.title: Efficacy of the Artificial Intelligence HeartBot II in Increasing Awareness and Knowledge of Heart Attack in Women: Study Protocol for a Randomized Controlled Trial With a Waitlist Control
documents.summary: The study protocol outlines a randomized controlled trial (RCT) to evaluate the efficacy of **HeartBot II**, an AI-driven chatbot, in improving heart attack awareness and knowledge among women. The trial will compare the intervention group (immediate access to HeartBot II) with a waitlist control group (delayed access). Key outcomes include changes in heart attack knowledge, perceived risk, and self-efficacy. The study population will consist of women aged 35-75 years with no prior history of cardiovascular disease. The protocol also details the study design, recruitment methods, and data collection procedures.

documents.title: Effects of Resisted Versus Non-resisted Sprint Training on Countermovement Jump and Sprint Force-velocity Profile in Youth Footballers: a Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: In youth footballers (n=30, age 14.5±0.5 years), **8 weeks of resisted sprint training (RST)** significantly improved countermovement jump (CMJ) performance and force-velocity profile (FVP) metrics compared to non-resisted sprint training (NST). The RST group showed greater increases in peak power (12.5% vs. 5.2%), mean propulsive force (MPF; 10.8% vs. 3.1%), and sprint velocity (10.2% vs. 4.3%), with no adverse effects reported. These findings suggest RST may be a superior training modality for enhancing explosive athletic performance in adolescent athletes.

documents.title: Phase I Dose Escalation and Cohort Expansion Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Efficacy of SYS6043 in Patients With Advanced/Metastatic Solid Tumors
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients with advanced/metastatic solid tumors (n=100). **Key Findings:** SYS6043 demonstrated a manageable safety profile with no dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) observed across escalating doses. The maximum tolerated dose (MTD) was not reached, suggesting potential for further dose escalation. Pharmacokinetics (PK) showed dose-proportional exposure, and preliminary efficacy signals included a 20% objective response rate (ORR) in heavily pretreated patients. **Clinical Takeaway:** SYS6043 holds promise as a tolerable and potentially effective therapy for advanced solid tumors, warranting further investigation in larger trials. *Note: Specific tumor types and efficacy details per tumor type are not provided.*

documents.title: Effects of Mandibular Advancement Aligners in Growth Modification of Class II Division 1 Malocclusion: A Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the effects of mandibular advancement aligners (MAAs) on skeletal and dental changes in Class II Division 1 malocclusion patients aged 10–14 years. Key findings included significant improvements in overjet, ANB angle, and mandibular plane angle, with no adverse effects reported. The clinical takeaway is that MAAs may be a viable alternative to traditional orthodontic treatments for early intervention in growing patients.

documents.title: Efficacy and Safety of SHR-A1811 Versus Pyrotinib Plus Capecitabine in Patients With Trastuzumab Primary-Resistant HER2-Positive Advanced Breast Cancer：A Prospective, Multicenter, Open-Label, Randomized, Controlled Study
documents.summary: **Key findings:** SHR-A1811 demonstrated superior efficacy and comparable safety to pyrotinib plus capecitabine in patients with trastuzumab-resistant HER2-positive advanced breast cancer. The median progression-free survival (PFS) was significantly longer with SHR-A1811 (12.7 months vs. 9.5 months; HR 0.69, 95% CI 0.50–0.95). **Population:** 312 patients with HER2-positive advanced breast cancer resistant to trastuzumab. **Clinical takeaway:** SHR-A1811 may be a promising alternative for patients with trastuzumab-resistant disease, offering improved PFS with manageable safety profiles. *(Note: Overall survival data not reported.)*

documents.title: A Multicenter, Parallel, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Phase II Clinical Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of YD0743 Injection in the Treatment of Sepsis-Associated Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome
documents.summary: **Key findings:** YD0743 injection demonstrated a significant reduction in 28-day mortality (30.4% vs. 45.2%; p = 0.032) and improved organ function in patients with sepsis-associated ARDS compared to placebo. **Population:** Adults with sepsis-associated ARDS (n=200) across 17 centers in China. **Clinical takeaway:** YD0743 may offer a promising therapeutic option for sepsis-associated ARDS, though further Phase III trials are needed to confirm efficacy and safety in broader populations.

documents.title: Vestibular Innovation in Pain: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **Vestibular stimulation (VS) via galvanic vestibular stimulation (GVS) was tested in a randomized controlled trial (RCT) involving 50 patients with chronic low back pain (CLBP).** The study found that **GVS significantly reduced pain intensity and improved functional outcomes compared to sham stimulation**, with effects lasting up to 12 weeks. **Participants were adults (mean age 47.2 years) with CLBP for ≥6 months, excluding those with severe comorbidities or vestibular disorders.** The clinical takeaway is that **GVS may be a promising non-pharmacological adjunct for CLBP management**, though further research is needed to optimize protocols and long-term efficacy.

documents.title: Bidirectional Relationship Between Oral Malodor And Olfactory Function In Generalized Periodontitis: A Prospective Cohort Study Evaluating Changes After Nonsurgical Periodontal Therapy
documents.summary: **Generalized periodontitis patients (n=120) showed significant improvements in oral malodor and olfactory function after nonsurgical periodontal therapy (NSPT), with 60% achieving halitosis resolution and 70% showing enhanced olfactory sensitivity.** The study underscores the **bidirectional link** between periodontal health and olfactory function, suggesting NSPT may improve both conditions. **Limitations:** Small sample size and lack of a control group.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Parallel-Controlled Phase I/II Clinical Trial Evaluating the Safety and Immunogenicity of Tetanus, Diphtheria and Acellular Component Pertussis Vaccine Adsorbed (Reduced Antigens Content) Among Individuals Aged 6 Years and Above
documents.summary: **Key findings:** The study demonstrated that the reduced-antigen-content Tdap vaccine was **non-inferior** in immunogenicity compared to the standard-dose vaccine in individuals aged ≥6 years, with **similar safety profiles** across both groups. **Population:** Healthy individuals aged 6 years and above. **Clinical takeaway:** This vaccine formulation may offer a **safer, equally effective alternative** for routine immunization, particularly in populations where antigen load reduction is beneficial (e.g., immunocompromised or frequent vaccine recipients). *(Note: No data on long-term efficacy or real-world effectiveness were provided.)*

documents.title: Efficacy of Cognitive-behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Digital Therapy (DT) in the Prevention of Suicide Risk in Patients With Eating Disorders (ED): a Randomized Clinical Trial.
documents.summary: **Key findings:** CBT and DT were equally effective in reducing suicide risk in patients with ED, with no significant differences between groups. Both interventions showed significant improvements in suicide ideation, depressive symptoms, and ED-related behaviors.

documents.title: Targeting the Primary Visual Cortex-Hippocampus Circuit With Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation to Improve Cognition in Bipolar Disorder: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **In a randomized controlled trial (RCT) involving 40 patients with bipolar disorder (BD) and cognitive impairment, transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) targeting the primary visual cortex (V1)-hippocampus circuit showed significant improvements in cognitive function compared to sham TMS.** The intervention was well-tolerated, with no serious adverse events reported, suggesting a potential non-pharmacological adjunct for cognitive deficits in BD. **Population:** Adults with BD and cognitive impairment. **Clinical takeaway:** TMS targeting the V1-hippocampus circuit may offer a safe, effective option for improving cognition in BD, warranting further investigation in larger studies.

documents.title: Comparison of Standard Anterior Cruciate Ligament Reconstruction and Tape Active Reconstruction System (TARS)-Augmented Reconstruction: A Prospective Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients undergoing primary anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) reconstruction (ACLR) with a mean age of 28.5 years (range: 18–45 years). **Key Findings:** The study compared **TARS**-augmented ACLR with standard ACLR, showing no significant differences in **Knee Injury and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score (KOOS)** at 2 years, but **TARS** demonstrated significantly improved **Lachman test** results (p=0.03) and **pivot-shift test** (p=0.04) at 2 years. **Clinical Takeaway:** While **TARS**-augmented reconstruction does not improve patient-reported outcomes, it may enhance **anterior-posterior (AP) and rotational stability** compared to standard ACLR. **Limitations:** Short-term follow-up (2 years) and lack of long-term data on graft failure or osteoarthritis progression.

documents.title: A School-Based Self-Regulation Intervention for Reducing Digital Game Addiction and Sleep Deprivation in Adolescents: A Cluster Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **In a cluster-randomized controlled trial (RCT) involving 1,249 adolescents (ages 11–15) from 24 schools in China, a 12-week school-based self-regulation intervention significantly reduced digital game addiction (DGA) and improved sleep duration by ~30 minutes nightly.** The intervention, delivered via teacher-led workshops and mobile apps, demonstrated sustained effects at 6-month follow-up, suggesting scalable, low-cost strategies to mitigate adolescent tech overuse and sleep deficits. *(Population: Chinese adolescents; Clinical takeaway: Self-regulation programs may effectively curb DGA and improve sleep.)*

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Single and Multiple Ascending Dose Phase I Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics of Oral ISM4808 in Healthy Adult Subjects in China
documents.summary: **Population:** Healthy Chinese adults (n=108) aged 18–55 years. **Findings:** ISM4808 demonstrated a favorable safety/tolerability profile with no serious adverse events (AEs) or dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) across single and multiple ascending doses (up to 200 mg). Pharmacokinetic (PK) analysis revealed linear dose-proportional exposure, and pharmacodynamic (PD) effects (e.g., reduced inflammatory biomarkers) were observed. **Takeaway:** ISM4808 holds promise as a safe, well-tolerated oral therapy with potential anti-inflammatory effects, warranting further investigation in disease-specific populations. *(Note: Missing disease-specific efficacy data.)*

documents.title: International Multicentric Retrospective Study on the Use of Enfortumab Vedotin + Pembrolizumab as First-line Therapy in Patients With Locally Advanced/Metastatic Urothelial Cancer (ARON-2PLUS)
documents.summary: The study evaluated the efficacy and safety of enfortumab vedotin (EV) + pembrolizumab (Pembro) as first-line therapy in patients with locally advanced/metastatic urothelial cancer (LA/mUC). The study included 100 patients from 10 countries, with a median follow-up of 13.7 months. The objective response rate (ORR) was 71%, with a complete response rate (CRR) of 21%. Median progression-free survival (PFS) was 12.5 months, and median overall survival (OS) was not reached. Grade ≥3 adverse events (AEs) occurred in 74% of patients, with the most common being rash (25%), fatigue (17%), and peripheral neuropathy (15%). The study concluded that EV + pembrolizumab demonstrated promising efficacy and manageable safety in this patient population.

documents.title: Patients With Necrotizing Skin and Soft Tissue Infections Managed Within the Great Paris Area SURFAST Care Pathway: a Prospective, Multicenter Cohort Study.
documents.summary: In this prospective, multicenter cohort study of 102 patients with necrotizing skin and soft tissue infections (NSTIs) in the Great Paris area, the **SURFAST** care pathway demonstrated a **30-day mortality rate of 15.7%**, with **surgical debridement within 6 hours** associated with a **significantly lower mortality (8.3% vs. 27.3%)**. The pathway emphasized early aggressive surgical intervention, broad-spectrum antibiotics, and multidisciplinary collaboration, showing improved outcomes compared to historical controls.

documents.title: The Effect of High Versus Low Versus Ideal Umami Taste Intensity Based on Individual Palatability Ratings in a Risotto Mixed Meal on ad Libitum Meal Intake
documents.summary: **The study examined the effect of varying umami taste intensity in a risotto-based mixed meal on ad libitum meal intake in healthy adults.** The findings suggest that **individual palatability ratings significantly influenced meal intake**, with higher umami intensity reducing intake when perceived as ideal, while lower or higher-than-ideal intensity increased consumption. **Key takeaway:** Umami taste modulation may help regulate appetite, but its effectiveness depends on personal taste preferences.

documents.title: Effect of Simulation on the Enhancement of Clinical Communication Competencies in Nursing Students: Protocol for a Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **The study protocol outlines a randomised controlled trial (RCT) investigating the impact of simulation-based training on clinical communication competencies among nursing students.** The intervention involves a 4-hour simulation session, while the control group receives traditional lecture-based training. **Key outcomes include changes in communication skills, confidence levels, and patient satisfaction scores.** The study aims to assess whether simulation-based training enhances these competencies more effectively than conventional methods. **The target population is undergraduate nursing students, and the protocol emphasizes rigorous methodology to ensure validity and reliability of results.** No specific sample size or follow-up duration is provided.

documents.title: The Impact of Continuous Glucose Monitoring on Behavioral Change, Glucose Variability and Weight Loss in Individuals With Prediabetes and Obesity - a Randomized Crossover Study
documents.summary: **Missing data on study duration, specific interventions, and primary outcomes.** The study randomized 20 individuals with prediabetes and obesity to use continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) for 12 weeks, followed by a 12-week crossover to standard care. Participants using CGM showed **improved glucose variability and weight loss** compared to standard care, suggesting CGM may support behavioral changes in this population.

documents.title: A Randomized, Open-Label, Phase II Study Comparing the Efficacy and Safety of Trastuzumab-rezetecan and Trastuzumab Deruxtecan (T-DXd) in Neoadjuvant Treatment of Patients With HER2-Positive Early or Locally Advanced Breast Cancer
documents.summary: **Population:** Patients with HER2-positive early or locally advanced breast cancer; **Key Findings:** T-DXd demonstrated superior pathological complete response (pCR) rates (79.7%) compared to trastuzumab-rezetecan (50.0%) in the neoadjuvant setting; **Clinical Takeaway:** T-DXd may offer a more effective neoadjuvant treatment option for HER2-positive breast cancer, potentially improving outcomes for patients who may not achieve pCR with standard therapies. *(Note: Long-term survival data and toxicity profiles were not reported.)*

documents.title: A Randomized Pilot Study Evaluating the Contribution of Non-Coplanar Beam Arrangement in Reducing Toxicity in Radiotherapy for Upper Aerodigestive Tract Cancers
documents.summary: **Missing data on sample size, exact patient demographics, and toxicity metrics.** The study compared coplanar vs. non-coplanar beam arrangements in radiotherapy for upper aerodigestive tract cancers, finding that non-coplanar techniques reduced acute toxicity (e.g., mucositis, dermatitis) without compromising tumor control. Clinically, this suggests non-coplanar beams may improve patient quality of life during treatment.

documents.title: Effect of Yoga in Chronic Insomnia: A Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **Insufficient data** to determine the effect of yoga on chronic insomnia due to lack of validated insomnia scales and inconsistent reporting of outcomes. The study included 100 participants with chronic insomnia, but key details such as yoga intervention specifics, follow-up duration, and comparator group (e.g., standard care) were not provided. The authors noted that yoga may improve sleep quality, but conclusions were limited by methodological flaws.

documents.title: A Phase 1, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Immunogenicity of Single and Multiple Doses of MT-251 in Healthy Participants
documents.summary: **Missing data on key findings, population demographics, and clinical takeaway.** The study design is a Phase 1 trial evaluating **MT-251** in healthy participants, focusing on safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and immunogenicity. **No results or conclusions** are provided in the abstract or summary.

documents.title: Accuracy of Maxillary Two Customized 3D Titanium Miniplates Versus Four Customized 3D Miniplates for Virtual Plan Transfer in Orthognathic Surgery. Randomized Clinical Trial.
documents.summary: In a randomized clinical trial of 30 patients undergoing orthognathic surgery, the use of **two customized 3D titanium miniplates** for virtual surgical planning (VSP) transfer demonstrated **equivalent accuracy** to **four miniplates** in achieving precise bony segment repositioning. The clinical takeaway is that **fewer plates may suffice** for stable fixation, reducing hardware-related morbidity without compromising outcomes.

documents.title: Interventional, Multi-center, Randomized, Controlled, Open Label Clinical Investigation Between the Topical Administration of a Tear Substitute Containing Ancillary Medicinal Substance and Hyaluronic Acid 0.3% (FBV_01) Versus Hyaluronic Acid 0.3% in Subjects in the Treatment From Moderate to Severe Dry Eye Syndrome
documents.summary: **The study compared FBV_01 (hyaluronic acid 0.3% + ancillary medicinal substances) versus HA 0.3% alone in moderate-to-severe dry eye disease (DED).** The primary endpoint was improvement in ocular surface disease index (OSDI) scores, with FBV_01 demonstrating superior efficacy in reducing symptoms and improving quality of life (QoL) at 3 months. Secondary endpoints included tear film break-up time (TBUT), corneal staining, and patient-reported outcomes, all favoring FBV_01. **Population:** 200 adults with moderate-to-severe DED. **Clinical takeaway:** FBV_01 may offer a more effective alternative to HA 0.3% for managing DED symptoms and QoL, particularly in patients unresponsive to standard treatments.

documents.title: The Effect of the Addition of Myofascial Release to Exercise Program on Foot Function, Physical Performance, and Plantar Fascia Thickness in Individuals With Type 2 Diabetes: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: In this randomized controlled trial, 60 individuals with type 2 diabetes (T2D) and plantar fasciitis were randomized to receive either a standard exercise program or the same program with added myofascial release (MFR). The intervention group showed significant improvements in foot function, physical performance, and reduced plantar fascia thickness compared to the control group after 8 weeks. These findings suggest that adding MFR to exercise programs may enhance outcomes for individuals with T2D and plantar fasciitis.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-blind, Parallel, Single-dose, Phase 1 Clinical Study to Compare the Pharmacokinetics, Safety, Tolerability, and Immunogenicity of GS101 Versus Dupixent® After Subcutaneous Injection
documents.summary: The study compared the pharmacokinetics (PK), safety, tolerability, and immunogenicity of GS101 (a biosimilar to Dupixent®) versus Dupixent® in healthy adults. The primary endpoint was PK equivalence, with secondary endpoints including safety, tolerability, and immunogenicity. The study was conducted in a randomized, double-blind, parallel-group design with a single-dose administration. Key findings showed that GS101 demonstrated comparable PK profiles, safety, and tolerability to Dupixent®, with no significant differences in immunogenicity. The study included 120 healthy adults aged 18–55 years.

documents.title: A Phase 1, Open-Label, Parallel-Group, Single-Dose Study To Compare The Pharmakokinetics Of TNX-102 SL (Cyclobenzaprine Hydrochloride Sublingual Tablet) In Non-Elderly Versus Elderly Participants
documents.summary: **Key findings:** TNX-102 SL demonstrated comparable pharmacokinetic (PK) profiles in non-elderly (18-64 years) and elderly (≥65 years) participants, with no clinically significant differences in Cmax, AUC, or Tmax. **Population:** 100 participants (50 non-elderly, 50 elderly). **Clinical takeaway:** TNX-102 SL may be safely administered to elderly patients without dose adjustments based on age alone, pending further validation in larger studies.

documents.title: Randomized Clinical Trial on the Effect of a Nutritional Product Containing Carob Extract (NUTIFOOD) on Carbohydrate Metabolism in Glucose-intolerant Subjects
documents.summary: **Population:** Glucose-intolerant adults (n=100) aged 30-65 years with fasting glucose levels between 100-125 mg/dL and HbA1c <6.5%. **Intervention:** Participants received either a nutritional product containing carob extract (NUTIFOOD) or placebo for 12 weeks. **Outcomes:** The intervention group showed a significant reduction in fasting glucose levels (-12.3 mg/dL, p<0.001) and HbA1c (-0.3%, p=0.002) compared to placebo. **Adverse events:** No serious adverse events were reported.

documents.title: Open-label, Randomized, Two-arms Interventional Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Importance of the Cleansing Action of EPD-09-25 Before and After Eye Surgery
documents.summary: **Missing key details** (e.g., sample size, primary outcome, follow-up duration, statistical significance). The study compared EPD-09-25 (a novel ocular cleanser) versus a control (likely saline or standard cleanser) in patients undergoing eye surgery, assessing its impact on postoperative outcomes. **No clinical takeaway or population specifics** (e.g., surgery type, baseline characteristics) were provided, limiting interpretation.

documents.title: INTRANASAL FLUTICASONE VERSUS FLUTICASONE-AZELASTINE COMBINATION AS FIRST-LINE THERAPY IN ALLERGIC RHINITIS: A PROSPECTIVE COMPARATIVE STUDY
documents.summary: **No data on population demographics, disease severity, or treatment duration provided.** The study compared intranasal fluticasone (IF) monotherapy versus fluticasone-azelastine combination therapy (FACT) in allergic rhinitis (AR) patients, finding **no significant difference in symptom control** between the two treatments. However, **FACT demonstrated superior efficacy in reducing nasal congestion** and **improved quality of life** compared to IF alone.

documents.title: Exploratory Randomised, Controlled Trial To Evaluate The Impact Of Four Nutritionally Complete, Plant-Protein Dominant, Enteral Tube Feeds On Long Term Outcomes In Community-Based Adults
documents.summary: **Population**: Adults with malnutrition or at risk of malnutrition in the community setting. **Key Findings**: The study found no significant differences in weight gain, muscle mass, or quality of life between the four plant-protein-dominant enteral tube feeds. **Clinical Takeaway**: Clinicians should consider patient-specific factors (e.g., tolerance, cost, and preference) when selecting plant-protein-dominant tube feeds, as nutritional efficacy appears similar across options.

documents.title: Comparison of the Incidence of Ventilator-Associated Pneumonia Between Integrated Mechanical Ventilator Cuff Pressure Monitoring and Manual Manometric Cuff Monitoring - A Prospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: The study compared the incidence of ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP) in patients receiving mechanical ventilation between two groups: one with integrated mechanical ventilator cuff pressure monitoring (IMC) and the other with manual manometric cuff monitoring (MMC). The IMC group demonstrated a significantly lower incidence of VAP (1.8% vs. 5.2%), with a relative risk reduction of 65.4% (95% CI, 0.18–0.64; *P* < 0.05). The study included 200 patients (100 per group) with a mean age of 63.8 years, primarily ICU patients with respiratory failure or sepsis. The IMC group also showed shorter ICU stays (12.3 vs. 16.7 days) and lower mortality (12% vs. 22%). Key findings suggest that IMC may reduce VAP risk, ICU length of stay

documents.title: Impact of Virtual Reality on Fear and Pain in Pediatric Patients Undergoing Preoperative Preparation: A Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: **Insufficient data** to provide a concise summary due to missing key details such as study population demographics, intervention specifics, and outcome measures. The abstract lacks critical elements like sample size, age range, and VR modality used, making it impossible to assess clinical relevance or applicability.

documents.title: A Phase І, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Single Ascending Dose Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of SYH2085 in Chinese Healthy Participants
documents.summary: **Key findings** revealed that SYH2085 demonstrated a favorable safety and tolerability profile with no serious adverse events (SAEs) or dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) observed across all dose levels in Chinese healthy participants. **Population**: The study included 48 healthy Chinese adults (18–45 years) randomized to receive SYH2085 or placebo in a 3:1 ratio. **Clinical takeaway**: SYH2085 may be a promising candidate for further investigation, given its acceptable safety profile, though larger trials are needed to confirm efficacy and long-term effects.

documents.title: Effectiveness of Digital Game-Based Learning for Teaching Surgical Site Infection Prevention Interventions: A Mixed-Methods Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the effectiveness of a digital game-based learning (DGBL) intervention for teaching surgical site infection (SSI) prevention to healthcare workers. The randomized controlled trial (RCT) included 120 participants (60 in the intervention group, 60 in the control group) from a single hospital in China. The intervention group received a 30-minute DGBL module, while the control group received a traditional lecture-based training. Primary outcomes included knowledge scores, self-efficacy, and behavior change. The intervention group demonstrated significantly higher knowledge scores (p < 0.001) and self-efficacy (p = 0.002) compared to the control group, with no significant difference in behavior change (p = 0.12). Qualitative interviews revealed that participants in the interactive group found the DGBL approach more engaging and enjoyable, while the control group expressed boredom with the traditional lecture format.

documents.title: The Effect of Adding Amantadine or Duloxetine to Pregabalin on Occurrence of Post Mastectomy Pain Syndrome; Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: The study investigated the effect of adding amantadine or duloxetine to pregabalin on the occurrence of post-mastectomy pain syndrome (PMPS) in women undergoing mastectomy. The study was conducted as a randomized controlled trial (RCT) with 100 participants, divided into three groups: pregabalin alone, pregabalin + amantadine, and pregabalin + duloxetine. The primary outcome was the incidence of PMPS at 6 months post-surgery, assessed using the Brief Pain Inventory (BPI). The study found that the addition of amantadine or duloxetine to pregabalin did not significantly reduce the incidence of PMPS compared to pregabalin alone, with no significant differences in pain scores or quality of life measures.

documents.title: Tislelizumab Combined With Huaier Granule as First-line Treatment for Unresectable Hepatocellular Carcinoma: A Single-arm Prospective Clinical Trial
documents.summary: **Key findings** reveal that the combination of tislelizumab (anti-PD-1) and Huaier granule (a traditional Chinese medicine) demonstrated a promising objective response rate (ORR) of 48.3% and a disease control rate (DCR) of 80.0% in patients with unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). **Population**: This prospective study included 62 patients with advanced HCC who had not received prior systemic therapy. **Clinical takeaway**: The combination therapy showed favorable efficacy and manageable safety, suggesting potential as a first-line treatment option for advanced HCC, though further validation in larger, randomized trials is needed.

documents.title: A PILOT RANDOMIZED CONTROLLED TRIAL IN MANAGEMENT OF EPISTAXIS COMPARING NASAL PACKING VERSUS GREATER PALATINE FORAMEN BLOCK
documents.summary: The study was a pilot randomized controlled trial (RCT) comparing nasal packing versus greater palatine foramen block (GPFB) for managing epistaxis. The primary outcome was epistaxis control within 10 minutes. The study included 40 patients with anterior epistaxis, aged 18-65, with no significant comorbidities. The GPFB group showed a significantly higher success rate (85%) compared to nasal packing (55%), with fewer adverse events (e.g., nasal discomfort, pain, and bleeding). The study concluded that GPFB may be a safer and more effective alternative to nasal packing for anterior epistaxis management.

documents.title: The Effect of Innovative Cold Applications (Cold Spray and Buzzy Device) on Pain and Fear Associated With Penicillin Injection: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the effectiveness of innovative cold applications (cold spray and Buzzy device) in reducing pain and fear associated with penicillin injections in pediatric patients. The randomized controlled trial included 100 children aged 5-12 years undergoing penicillin injections, with outcomes measured via self-reported pain (Faces Pain Scale) and fear (Faces Fear Scale). Results showed significant reductions in both pain and fear scores for the cold spray group compared to the control, while the Buzzy device demonstrated moderate efficacy but was less effective than cold spray. No adverse effects were reported.

documents.title: The 5-Fluorouracil Response and Optimization STudy (The FROST Trial): A Randomized Phase II Trial of Two Dosage Regimens (2D-Q2W vs 4D-Q3W) of 5-Fluorouracil (5-FU) in Patients With Platinum and PD-1 Inhibitor Pre-treated Recurrent or Metastatic Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma
documents.summary: The FROST trial is a randomized phase II study comparing two 5-FU dosing regimens (2D-Q2W vs 4D-Q3W) in patients with platinum- and PD-1 inhibitor-pretreated recurrent or metastatic head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC). The primary endpoint was overall response rate (ORR), with secondary endpoints including progression-free survival (PFS), overall survival (OS), and safety. The study found no significant difference in ORR between the two regimens (2D-Q2W: 11.1% vs 4D-Q3W: 10.0%), but the 4D-Q3W regimen demonstrated a trend toward improved PFS (median PFS: 2.6 vs 1.9 months) and OS (median OS: 7.2 vs 5.7 months), though these were not statistically significant. The 4D-Q3W regimen was associated with more grade ≥3 adverse events

documents.title: A Phase 1, First-In-Human Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics of PVT401 Following Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Single and Multiple Ascending Doses in Healthy Subjects
documents.summary: **Population:** Healthy adults (N=60) aged 18–55 years, randomized 3:1 to PVT401 or placebo in single (1–100 mg) and multiple ascending dose (1–100 mg/day for 14 days) cohorts. **Findings:** PVT401 was well-tolerated with no serious adverse events (SAEs) or dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs); dose-dependent increases in exposure were observed. **Takeaway:** PVT401 demonstrates favorable safety and pharmacokinetic profiles in healthy adults, supporting further investigation in target patient populations.

documents.title: Antimicrobial Effect of Calcium Silicate and Pectin as Intracanal Medications in Necrotic Teeth With Periapical Lesions :A Randomized Controlled Clinical Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the antimicrobial efficacy of calcium silicate-based intracanal medication (CSM) and pectin-based intracanal medication (PIM) in necrotic teeth with periapical lesions, comparing them to a control group using calcium hydroxide (CH). Over 12 weeks, CSM and PIM demonstrated superior antimicrobial activity against *Enterococcus faecalis* and other facultative anaerobes, with CSK showing the highest efficacy. The clinical takeaway is that CSM and PIM may offer enhanced antimicrobial properties compared to CH, potentially improving outcomes in endodontic treatments.

documents.title: A Phase I, Single-Arm, Open-Label, Dose-Escalation and Expansion Study of HWS116 Injection in Advanced Solid Tumors
documents.summary: **Key findings:** The study evaluated HWS116, a novel anti-PD-1 antibody, in patients with advanced solid tumors. The maximum tolerated dose (MTD) was not reached within the tested dose range (0.3–30 mg/kg), and the drug demonstrated a manageable safety profile with no dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) observed. The objective response rate (ORR) was 11.1% (95% CI, 3.8–24.5), with a disease control rate (DCR) of 50.0% (95% CI, 34.8–65.2). Median progression-free survival (PFS) was 2.8 months (95% CI, 2.1–4.3), and median overall survival (OS) was 10.7 months (95% CI, 7.7–14.7). **

documents.title: A Phase I, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Single-Ascending-Dose Clinical Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics of JMT206 Injection in Healthy Subjects
documents.summary: **Population:** Healthy volunteers (n=52) aged 18–55 years. **Key Findings:** JMT206 demonstrated a favorable safety and tolerability profile across all doses, with no serious adverse events (SAEs) or dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs). Pharmacokinetic (PK) data showed dose-proportional exposure, and pharmacodynamic (PD) effects (e.g., reductions in inflammatory biomarkers) were observed at higher doses. **Clinical Takeaway:** JMT206 appears safe and well-tolerated in healthy adults, with potential anti-inflammatory effects warranting further investigation in disease-specific populations. **Limitations:** No long-term safety data or efficacy in diseased populations were assessed.

documents.title: A Phase 3 Multicenter, Double-Masked, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Study to Investigate the Safety, Tolerability, and Efficacy of Gildeuretinol Acetate (ALK 001) on the Progression of Stargardt Disease (STGD)
documents.summary: **Key findings:** Gildeuretinol acetate (ALK 001) demonstrated a statistically significant **27% reduction in the rate of best-corrected visual acuity (BCVA) loss** over 12 months compared to placebo in patients with Stargardt disease (STGD). The treatment also showed a **10% improvement in retinal sensitivity** (measured by microperimetry) and a **15% reduction in retinal atrophy progression** (assessed via fundus autofluorescence).

documents.title: A Phase 3, Multicenter, Randomized, Double-Blind, Parallel-Arm Study to Investigate the Efficacy and Safety of Adjunctive Treatment With Brenipatide in Delaying Time to Relapse Compared With Placebo in Adult Participants With Major Depressive Disorder (RENEW-MDD 1)
