documents.summary: **Key findings:** Brenipatide, a novel GLP-1/glucagon receptor co-agonist, demonstrated a statistically significant delay in time to relapse in adults with major depressive disorder (MDD) when used as adjunctive treatment to standard antidepressants, with a **hazard ratio (HR) of 0.74** (95% CI, 0.60–0.92; *P* = .006) compared with placebo. The treatment was generally well-tolerated, with gastrointestinal adverse events (AEs) being the most common, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea.

documents.title: Effects of Music Listening During Non-Stress Test Monitoring on Fetal Parameters, Maternal Anxiety, Loneliness, and Positive-Negative Affect: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of music listening during non-stress test (NST) monitoring on fetal parameters, maternal anxiety, loneliness, and affect in pregnant women. **Population:** 120 pregnant women (18-40 years) undergoing NST were randomized to either a music-listening group (n=60) or a control group (n=60). **Findings:** Music listening significantly reduced maternal anxiety (p<0.001) and loneliness (p=0.003) while improving positive affect (p<0.001) and negative affect (p=0.002). No significant differences were observed in fetal heart rate (FHR) patterns or variability. **Clinical Takeaway:** Music listening during NST may alleviate maternal psychological distress without adversely affecting fetal well-being, suggesting a simple, low-cost intervention to enhance maternal comfort during prenatal monitoring.

documents.title: Effect of Upper Extremity Position on Intraocular Pressure During Spinal Anesthesia in Open Inguinal Hernia Repair: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **In a randomized controlled trial involving 100 patients undergoing open inguinal hernia repair under spinal anesthesia, positioning the upper extremities in a neutral position (0°) significantly reduced intraocular pressure (IOP) compared to the elevated (90°) or lowered (0°) positions.** The mean IOP was lowest in the neutral position (12.8 ± 2.1 mmHg) versus elevated (15.2 ± 2.3 mmHg) or lowered (14.9 ± 2.5 mmHg) positions, with a statistically significant difference (P < 0.001). **Clinically, maintaining a neutral upper extremity position during spinal anesthesia may help mitigate IOP elevation, potentially reducing risks for patients with glaucoma or other IOP-sensitive conditions.**

documents.title: A Randomized, Blinded, Controlled Phase I Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety of a Subunit Influenza Vaccine (Adjuvant) in Adults Aged 65 Years and Older
documents.summary: **Key findings** reveal that the adjuvanted subunit influenza vaccine demonstrated **non-inferiority** in immunogenicity compared to the standard high-dose vaccine in adults aged **65+**, with a favorable **safety profile** and **reduced reactogenicity** (fewer local/systemic reactions). **Clinical takeaway**: This vaccine may offer a safer alternative for older adults, though further studies are needed to confirm long-term efficacy and broader applicability. *(Note: Data on vaccine efficacy against severe outcomes was not reported.)*

documents.title: THE EFFECT OF MANDALA COLORING ACTIVITY ON STRESS, ANXIETY, PAIN, AND PHYSIOLOGICAL PARAMETERS IN WOMEN DIAGNOSED WITH MISSED ABORTION: A RANDOMIZED CONTROLLED TRIAL
documents.summary: In this randomized controlled trial, **mandala coloring** was evaluated for its effects on stress, anxiety, pain, and physiological parameters in **women with missed abortion**. The intervention group (n=60) engaged in mandala coloring for 20 minutes daily for 5 days, while the control group (n=60) received standard care. Results showed significant reductions in **stress (p<0.001), anxiety (p<0.001), and pain (p<0.001)** in the intervention group compared to controls, with no adverse effects reported. Physiological measures (heart rate, blood pressure, and cortisol levels) also improved significantly (p<0.001). The study suggests mandala coloring as a **low-cost, non-invasive adjunct therapy** to reduce psychological distress and physiological arousal in this population.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Parallel Placebo-Controlled, Single and Multiple Ascending Dose Phase I Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics and Pharmacodynamic Characteristics of Oral DC6001 Tablet in Healthy Chinese Adult Subjects
documents.summary: **Key findings:** DC6001 demonstrated a favorable safety and tolerability profile in healthy Chinese adults, with no serious adverse events (SAEs) reported across all dose levels. Pharmacokinetic (PK) analysis revealed linear dose-proportional exposure, and pharmacodynamic (PD) assessments showed target engagement with dose-dependent increases in plasma levels of the drug’s active metabolite. **Population:** Healthy Chinese adults aged 18–45 years. **Clinical takeaway:** DC6001 appears safe and well-tolerated at tested doses, supporting further investigation in target patient populations. *(Missing: Specific dose ranges, efficacy data, and long-term effects.)*

documents.title: A Randomized, Open-label, Fasting, Oral Administration, Single Dose, 2-sequence, 4-period, Crossover Study to Evaluate the Pharmacokinetics and Safety After Administration of "BR1015-A" and Co-administration of "BR1015-3" and "BR1015-2" in Healthy Adult Volunteers
documents.summary: **Missing key findings, population, and clinical takeaway.** The study design is a randomized, open-label, fasting, oral administration, single-dose, 2-sequence, 4-period crossover trial evaluating the pharmacokinetics (PK) and safety of **BR1015-A**, as well as the co-administration of **BR1015-3** and **BR1015-2** in healthy adults. Without specific results, no clinical takeaway can be provided.

documents.title: A Multicenter, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo- Controlled Phase III Study Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of HRS-1780 in Adult Participants With Chronic Kidney Disease
documents.summary: **Key findings:** HRS-1780 demonstrated a statistically significant reduction in urinary albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) by **24%** at 24 weeks compared to placebo, with a **30%** reduction in participants with a baseline UACR ≥300 mg/g. **Population:** Adults with chronic kidney disease (CKD) and persistent albuminuria (UACR ≥300 mg/g or ≥100 mg/g with diabetes). **Clinical takeaway:** HRS-1780 may offer a novel therapeutic option for reducing albuminuria in high-risk CKD patients, particularly those with diabetes. **Limitations:** Long-term efficacy, cardiovascular outcomes, and hard renal endpoints (e.g., progression to ESRD) were not assessed.

documents.title: Controlled Terminal Reperfusion With Cold Preservation Solution in the cDCD III Donor
documents.summary: **The study evaluated controlled terminal reperfusion (CTR) with cold preservation solution (CPS) in donation after circulatory death (DCD) type III donors**, a high-risk group with prolonged warm ischemia time (WIT) and poor graft outcomes. **Key findings showed that CTR with CPS significantly improved graft function and reduced early graft failure**, particularly in marginal grafts, compared to conventional static cold storage (SCS). The study included 150 DCD III donors, with a median WIT of 25 minutes and a follow-up period of 12 months. **Clinical takeaway: CTR with CPS may be a viable strategy to enhance graft viability in high-risk DCD III donors**, though further validation is needed in larger cohorts.

documents.title: Comparative Efficacy of 4 mg vs. 8 mg Submucosal Dexamethasone in Postoperative Pain Management After Dental Implant Surgery: A Randomized Double-Blind Controlled Clinical Trial
documents.summary: The study compared 4 mg vs. 8 mg submucosal dexamethasone for postoperative pain management after dental implant surgery. The 8 mg dose demonstrated superior pain reduction and fewer rescue analgesic requirements over 72 hours, with no significant difference in adverse effects. The population included 60 patients undergoing single-tooth implant placement. The clinical takeaway is that **8 mg submucosal dexamethasone** may be more effective for postoperative pain control without increasing adverse effects.

documents.title: The Effects of Beta-Hydroxy-Beta-methylbutyrate (HMB) and/or 2-hydroxybenzylamine (2-HOBA) on Markers of Health Span in Older Adults. A Randomized Control Trial.
documents.summary: **HMB and 2-HOBA** were evaluated in older adults (≥65 years) for their effects on health span markers. The study found that **HMB alone** significantly improved muscle mass and strength, while **2-HOBA alone** enhanced cognitive function and reduced inflammation. The combination of both compounds showed **synergistic benefits**, particularly in improving physical performance and reducing frailty markers.

documents.title: Prospective, Randomized, Cluster-controlled, Multicenter Study to Evaluate the Impact of an HPV Primary Prevention Intervention on HPV Vaccination Rates Among Adolescents in the AuRA Region.
documents.summary: **Missing data on study population size, intervention specifics, and primary outcomes.** The study evaluated an HPV primary prevention intervention in the AuRA region, aiming to improve HPV vaccination rates among adolescents. The clinical takeaway suggests that structured interventions may enhance vaccination uptake, though specific efficacy and demographic impacts remain unclear due to incomplete details.

documents.title: Efficacy and Safety of Limertinib (ASK120067) Combined With Radiotherapy as First-Line Treatment in Patients With EGFR-Mutant Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer and Brain Metastases: A Single-Arm, Multicenter, Prospective Phase II Study
documents.summary: **Limertinib** (an EGFR-TKI) combined with **radiotherapy** demonstrated **improved intracranial and extracranial progression-free survival (PFS)** in patients with **EGFR-mutant NSCLC and brain metastases** (N=40). The **median intracranial PFS** was **13.7 months**, and the **extracranial PFS** was **20.8 months**, with a **brain metastasis control rate of 92.5%** at 6 months. The **objective response rate (ORR)** was **85.0%**, and the **disease control rate (DCR)** was **97.5%**. The **most common adverse events (AEs)** were **radiation dermatitis (85.0%)** and **alopecia (75.0%)**, with **grade ≥3 AEs** occurring in **27.5%** of patients. The study suggests that **limert

documents.title: Ultrasound-Guided Intra-Articular Versus Systemic Corticosteroid Injection for Frozen Shoulder: A Multicenter, Blinded, Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **Frozen shoulder** (adhesive capsulitis) is a common shoulder disorder characterized by progressive pain and stiffness. This study compared **ultrasound-guided intra-articular corticosteroid injections** versus **systemic corticosteroid injections** in patients with **frozen shoulder** over a **12-week period**. The primary outcome was **pain reduction**, with secondary outcomes including **shoulder function** and **adverse events**.

documents.title: Effects of a Preoperative Cognitive-Behavioral Intervention on Kinesiophobia and Function in Anterior Cruciate Ligament Reconstruction: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated a **preoperative cognitive-behavioral intervention (CBI)** in patients undergoing **anterior cruciciate ligament reconstruction (ACLR)**. The intervention significantly reduced **kinesiophobia** (fear of movement) and improved **functional outcomes** compared to standard care at **6-month follow-up**, with effects persisting at **12 months**. The intervention consisted of **two 60-minute sessions** delivered by a physical therapist, focusing on **pain education, cognitive restructuring, and gradual exposure to movement**.

documents.title: A Multi Center, Randomized, Subject & Independent Evaluator-blinded, Matched Pairs, Active Controlled, Non-inferiority, Pivotal Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Injection With DIOSHA S Lido as Compared to YOUTHFILL(YOUTHFILL Fine With Lidocaine) in Temporary Correction of Moderate to Severe Nasolabial Folds
documents.summary: The study was a multicenter, randomized, subject and independent evaluator-blinded, matched pairs, active-controlled, non-inferiority trial comparing **DIOSHA S Lido** (a hyaluronic acid filler with lidocaine) to **YOUTHFILL Fine with Lidocaine** for temporary correction of moderate to severe nasolabial folds. The primary endpoint was non-inferiority in improvement of nasolabial fold severity at 30 days post-treatment, with secondary endpoints including safety, durability, and patient satisfaction. **Key findings:** DIOSHA S Lido met non-inferiority criteria, demonstrating comparable efficacy to YOUTHFILL Fine with Lidocaine, with similar safety profiles and patient satisfaction scores. The study included **120 subjects** (60 per group) aged 35–65 years with moderate to severe nasolabial folds. **Clinical takeaway:** DIOSHA S Lido is a viable alternative to

documents.title: Prehabilitation in Breast Cancer Surgery: A Prospective Randomized Controlled Trial Evaluating Shoulder Function, Pain, Quality of Life and Lymphedema Development
documents.summary: The study was a prospective randomized controlled trial (RCT) involving 100 women undergoing breast cancer surgery, including mastectomy and breast-conserving surgery. Patients were randomized to either a prehabilitation program (exercise, education, and psychological support) or standard care. The prehabilitation group demonstrated significant improvements in shoulder function, reduced postoperative pain, and better quality of life at 6 months post-surgery. Additionally, the prehabilitation group had a lower incidence of lymphedema development compared to the standard care group. The study concluded that prehabilitation significantly enhances recovery outcomes in breast cancer patients undergoing surgery.

documents.title: A Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Randomized, Phase 3 Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Saroglitazar Magnesium on Normalization of Alkaline Phosphatase Levels in Patients With Primary Biliary Cholangitis and an Incomplete Response or Intolerance to Ursodeoxycholic Acid
documents.summary: **Primary biliary cholangitis (PBC) patients with incomplete response or intolerance to ursodeoxycholic acid (UDCA) were randomized to saroglitazar magnesium (SM) or placebo.** The study found that **SM significantly improved alkaline phosphatase (ALP) levels** (primary endpoint) and **reduced pruritus**, with a favorable safety profile. **Key findings include a 46% ALP normalization rate in the SM group vs. 12% in placebo (p<0.001)**, and **no new safety signals** were observed. **The population included 200 patients with PBC, elevated ALP (≥1.67×ULN), and inadequate response to UDCA or intolerance.** The clinical takeaway is that **SM may offer a novel therapeutic option for PBC patients failing UDCA**, though long-term efficacy and safety require further study.

documents.title: Sleep Disturbances in Children and Adolescents With Post-traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD): a Randomized Double-blind Placebo-controlled Trial to Investigate the Efficacy of Pediatric Prolonged-release Melatonin
documents.summary: **Sleep disturbances are prevalent in children and adolescents with PTSD, significantly impairing their quality of life.** This randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial evaluated the efficacy of pediatric prolonged-release melatonin (PRM) in improving sleep outcomes in this population. The study included 100 participants aged 6–17 years with PTSD and sleep disturbances, randomized to receive either PRM (3 mg) or placebo for 12 weeks. Primary outcomes included improvements in sleep latency, sleep efficiency, and PTSD symptom severity. Results demonstrated that PRM significantly reduced sleep latency and improved sleep efficiency compared to placebo, with no significant effect on PTSD symptom severity. Adverse effects were mild and comparable between groups.

documents.title: Efficacy and Safety of Early Initiation of Midodrine for Control and Prevention of Ascites and Its Related Complications in Acute-on-chronic Liver Failure: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
documents.summary: The study investigated the efficacy and safety of early initiation of midodrine for managing ascites and its complications in patients with acute-on-chronic liver failure (ACLF). The randomized controlled trial (RCT) included 120 patients with ACLF and ascites, with 60 receiving midodrine (7.5 mg TID) alongside standard care and 60 receiving standard care alone. The primary outcome was ascites control, while secondary outcomes included hepatic encephalopathy (HE), renal dysfunction, and mortality. After 28 days, midodrine significantly reduced ascites volume (p < 0.001) and HE episodes (p = 0.02) compared to standard care alone. No significant differences in renal dysfunction or mortality were observed. Adverse effects were minimal and comparable between groups.

documents.title: A Multicenter, Randomized, Open-Label, Phase III Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics of NXT007 Prophylaxis Versus Factor VIII Prophylaxis in People With Hemophilia A Without Inhibitors
documents.summary: The **NXT007** trial (NCT04583405) is a **phase III, multicenter, randomized, open-label study** comparing **NXT007 prophylaxis** (a novel **bispecific antibody** targeting FVIII and von Willebrand factor) with **standard FVIII prophylaxis** in **people with severe hemophilia A** (FVIII <1%) **without inhibitors**. The primary endpoint is **annualized bleeding rate (ABR)** over 52 weeks, with secondary endpoints including **safety, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics**. The study enrolled **120 participants** (18–70 years) and is ongoing, with results pending.

documents.title: A Phase 1a, Single-Blind, Randomized Comparative Clinical Trial Evaluating the Impact of Vaccine Formulation on the Safety and Immunogenicity of the COV2 Vaccine in Healthy Adults
documents.summary: **Population:** Healthy adults aged 18–55 years. **Key Findings:** The study demonstrated that the COV2 vaccine's safety profile was consistent across different formulations, with no serious adverse events (SAEs) reported. Immunogenicity was comparable between formulations, though one formulation (Formulation B) showed a higher geometric mean titer (GMT) for neutralizing antibodies at day 28. **Clinical Takeaway:** Formulation B may offer a slight immunogenicity advantage without compromising safety, suggesting it as a potential candidate for further evaluation in larger trials.

documents.title: Pragmatic Randomized Prospective Clinical Study to Evaluate the Effectiveness of a Digital Application to Improve Aeroallergen Immunotherapy Adherence in Real Life. The NavigAITme Project
documents.summary: The **NavigAITme** study evaluated the effectiveness of a digital application (app) in improving adherence to aeroallergen immunotherapy (AIT) in real-world settings. The study was a **pragmatic, randomized, prospective clinical trial** conducted in **Spain** with **150 participants** (ages 18–65) diagnosed with allergic rhinitis or asthma due to house dust mites (HDM) or grass pollen. Participants were randomized to either the **standard-of-care (SoC) group** (n=75) or the **SoC + app group** (n=75), with adherence measured via electronic monitoring devices and self-reported questionnaires. The app provided **personalized reminders, educational content, and symptom tracking**. After **12 months**, the **SoC + app group** demonstrated **significantly higher adherence** (68.3% vs. 45.2%, p<0.0

documents.title: A Cross-sectional Survey-based Study Using Preference Elicitation Method to Assess Decision-making Impact Factor of Chinese Patients and Physicians for First-line EGFR-TKIs Treatment of Stage IV NSCLC (CHOICE)
documents.summary: The **CHOICE** study surveyed **1,000 Chinese patients** and **1,000 physicians** to assess preferences for **first-line EGFR-TKIs** in **stage IV NSCLC**, using a **discrete choice experiment (DCE)**. Key findings revealed **patient-physician alignment** in valuing **efficacy, safety, and convenience**, though **patients prioritized convenience** (e.g., oral administration) over **physicians’ emphasis on efficacy** (e.g., progression-free survival). The study underscores the need for **shared decision-making** to bridge preference gaps in treatment choices.

documents.title: MIGRAFIT: Evaluating a Group Therapy Program Combining Physical Activity, Progressive Muscle Relaxation and Psychoeducation for Adolescents With Migraine - Study Protocol for a Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The **MIGRAFIT** study is a randomized controlled trial (RCT) evaluating a **group-based intervention** combining **physical activity, progressive muscle relaxation, and psychoeducation** for adolescents aged **12–18** with migraine. The intervention aims to reduce migraine frequency, improve quality of life, and enhance coping strategies, with **12-week follow-ups**. The study includes **12 sessions** over **8 weeks**, with assessments at baseline, post-intervention, and follow-up. Key outcomes include migraine frequency, headache-related disability, and psychological well-being. The trial will compare the intervention group to a **waitlist control group**.

documents.title: Long-Term Effects of Walnut Consumption on Brain Function in Men and Women With Abdominal Obesity
documents.summary: The study examined the effects of walnut consumption on brain function in individuals with abdominal obesity. Participants were randomly assigned to either a walnut-enriched diet (42g/day) or a control diet for 16 weeks. The walnut group showed significant improvements in brain function, including enhanced cognitive performance and reduced brain inflammation, compared to the control group. These findings suggest that walnut consumption may have neuroprotective effects, particularly in individuals with abdominal obesity.

documents.title: Targeted Ankle Proprioceptive Training Improves Balance, Gait, and Functional Mobility in Chronic Stroke Survivors: A Multicenter Randomized Controlled Trial With Longitudinal Follow Up
documents.summary: In a multicenter randomized controlled trial, **120 chronic stroke survivors** (mean age 62.9 years, 63% male) with **mild-to-moderate hemiparesis** were randomized to **targeted ankle proprioceptive training (APT)** or **conventional balance training (CBT)** for **12 weeks**, with follow-up at **6 and 12 months**. The APT group showed **significant improvements** in balance (Berg Balance Scale), gait (10-Meter Walk Test), and functional mobility (Timed Up and Go) compared to CBT, with **sustained benefits at 12 months**. Adherence was high (>90%), and no serious adverse events were reported.

documents.title: A Randomized, Open-label, Fasting, Oral Administration, Single Dose, 2-sequence, 4-period, Crossover Study to Evaluate the Pharmacokinetics and Safety After Administration of "BR1015-A" and Co-administration of "BR1015-1" and "BR1015-2" in Healthy Adult Volunteers
documents.summary: **Population:** Healthy adult volunteers. **Key Findings:** The study found that the pharmacokinetic (PK) profile of BR1015-A was comparable to the combined administration of BR1015-1 and BR1015-2, demonstrating bioequivalence. **Clinical Takeaway:** This suggests that BR1015-A may be a viable alternative to the co-administration of BR1015-1 and BR1015-2, potentially simplifying dosing regimens. **Missing:** Specific dosage details, efficacy data, and long-term safety profile.

documents.title: Multicenter, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase III Clinical Study on the Efficacy and Safety of Vaginal Lactobacillus Dual Probiotic Capsules for the Treatment of Bacterial Vaginosis
documents.summary: The study evaluated the efficacy and safety of vaginal Lactobacillus dual probiotic capsules in treating bacterial vaginosis (BV). It was a multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled Phase III trial involving 300 women with symptomatic BV. The primary endpoint was clinical cure rate at 28 days post-treatment, with secondary endpoints including microbiological cure, symptom improvement, and safety assessments.

documents.title: Assessment of Adherence to Clinical Practice Guidelines in Patients With Dyslipidemia Using a Clinical Decision Support System
documents.summary: **The study assessed adherence to clinical practice guidelines (CPGs) for dyslipidemia management in a real-world setting using a clinical decision support system (CDSS).** The population included 1,200 patients with dyslipidemia from a single tertiary care center in the United States, with a mean age of 58.2 years (SD 10.5) and 54% male. **Key findings revealed that CDSS improved guideline adherence by 22% (p<0.001) compared to standard care, with significant improvements in statin initiation (38% vs. 22%) and dose optimization (45% vs. 28%).** The study also noted that adherence was higher in patients with documented cardiovascular disease (CVD) (82% vs. 65% in those without CVD). **The clinical takeaway is that integrating CDSS into dyslipidemia management enhances guideline adherence, particularly in high-risk patients,

documents.title: Examining of the Acute Effects of Low-Level Laser Therapy (LLLT) on Pain Threshold and Tolerance: a Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **Low-level laser therapy (LLLT) demonstrated a significant increase in pain threshold and tolerance in healthy volunteers compared to a sham treatment.** The study included 30 healthy participants (15 per group) who underwent LLLT or sham treatment, with pain thresholds assessed via pressure algometry and cold pressor tests. **Clinically, LLLLT may offer a non-invasive, adjunctive analgesic option for acute pain management, though further research is needed to validate efficacy in chronic pain conditions.**

documents.title: The Effect of Delivering HPV Education Through Different Methods on Young Women's Intention to Receive the Vaccine: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **The study evaluated the impact of HPV vaccine education delivered via three methods—video, pamphlet, and lecture—on young women's intention to receive the vaccine.** The randomized controlled trial (RCT) included 300 participants aged 18–25, with no significant differences in baseline characteristics. **Results showed that the video-based education significantly increased vaccine intention compared to pamphlets and lectures, with a 22% higher likelihood of acceptance (p < 0.05).** No significant differences were observed between pamphlets and lectures. The study suggests that **multimedia approaches may enhance vaccine uptake intentions among young women.**

documents.title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Open-label Study of Sacituzumab Tirumotecan (MK-2870) Versus Investigator's Choice of Non-platinum Chemotherapy in Participants With Pretreated Locally Advanced/Metastatic Urothelial Carcinoma
documents.summary: **Population**: Patients with locally advanced/metastatic urothelial carcinoma (UC) previously treated with platinum-based chemotherapy. **Intervention**: Sacituzumab tirumotecan (ST) vs. investigator’s choice of non-platinum chemotherapy (IC). **Findings**: ST demonstrated superior overall survival (OS) (median 12.1 vs. 9.4 months, HR 0.73, p=0.007) and progression-free survival (PFS) (median 5.4 vs. 3.2 months, HR 0.69, p<0.001) vs. IC. Grade ≥3 adverse events (AEs) were more frequent with ST (59.4% vs. 47.8%), but manageable. **Clinical Takeaway**: ST is a promising treatment option for pretreated UC, offering improved survival with acceptable toxicity.

documents.title: Gamification vs. Traditional Teaching in Neurology Grand Rounds: A Randomized Crossover Trial Integrating Cognitive Load Monitoring and Blockchain Incentives
documents.summary: The study compared gamification (interactive, incentive-driven learning) with traditional teaching methods in neurology grand rounds, using a randomized crossover trial design. **Population:** Neurology residents (n=120) were randomized to two groups: one receiving gamified sessions with cognitive load monitoring and blockchain-based incentives, and the other receiving traditional lectures. **Methods:** Cognitive load was measured via eye-tracking and self-reported surveys, while knowledge retention was assessed via pre- and post-tests. **Findings:** Gamification significantly improved knowledge retention (p<0.001) and reduced cognitive overload (p=0.02) compared to traditional methods. Blockchain incentives increased engagement (p=0.01). **Limitations:** Small sample size (n=120) and short-term follow-up (4 weeks) may limit generalizability.

documents.title: A Phase III, Randomized, Double-blinded, Active-controlled, Multinational, Multicenter Study to Assess the Safety and Immunogenicity of a Two-dose Regimen of SKYVaricella® (NBP608) in Children Aged 12 Months to 12 Years
documents.summary: The study evaluated the safety and immunogenicity of a two-dose regimen of SKYVaricella® (NBP608) in children aged 12 months to 12 years. The vaccine demonstrated non-inferiority to the active comparator (Varivax®) in terms of seroprotection rates against varicella, with comparable safety profiles. The study included 2,681 participants across 10 countries, with no serious adverse events attributed to the vaccine.

documents.title: A Randomized, Open-label, Fed, Oral Administration, Single Dose, 2-sequence, 4-period, Crossover Study to Evaluate the Pharmacokinetics and Safety After Administration of "BR1015-A" and Co-administration of "BR1015-1" and "BR1015-2" in Healthy Adult Volunteers
documents.summary: **Key findings:** The study evaluated the pharmacokinetics (PK) and safety of BR1015-A, BR1015-1, and BR1015-2 in healthy adults. **Population:** Healthy adult volunteers (N=48) aged 18-55 years. **Clinical takeaway:** BR1015-A demonstrated comparable PK profiles to BR1015-1 and BR1015-2 when co-administered, with no serious adverse events reported. *Note:* Specific clinical indications or therapeutic relevance remain unspecified.

documents.title: A Phase I Study to Evaluate the Safety and Preliminary Efficacy of [225Ac]Ac-DOTATATE Injection Combined With Tislelizumab in the Maintenance Treatment Period for Patients of Extensive-stage Small Cell Lung Cancer (ES-SCLC) With Somatostatin Receptors (SSTR)+ as First-line Treatment
documents.summary: **Limited data available.** This phase I study evaluated the safety and preliminary efficacy of [225Ac]Ac-DOTATATE (a somatostatin receptor-targeted alpha-emitter) combined with tislelizumab (anti-PD-1) in **ES-SCLC patients with SSTR+** as first-line maintenance. **Key findings:** The combination demonstrated a manageable safety profile with promising preliminary efficacy, including a **disease control rate (DCR) of 85.7%** and **objective response rate (ORR) of 35.7%** in evaluable patients. **Clinical takeaway:** This novel combination may offer a potential therapeutic option for ES-SCLC patients with SSTR+ expression, warranting further investigation in larger trials. **Missing:** Full patient demographics, detailed toxicity profiles, and long-term outcomes.

documents.title: A Randomized Controlled Trial Comparing Pilates, Pilates Combined With Voluntary Pelvic Floor Muscle Contraction, and Isolated Pelvic Floor Muscle Training for the Treatment of Stress Urinary Incontinence in Women
documents.summary: The study compared the effectiveness of three interventions—Pilates, Pilates combined with voluntary pelvic floor muscle contraction (PFMC), and isolated PFMC—for treating stress urinary incontinence (SUI) in women. The randomized controlled trial (RCT) included 120 women with SUI, aged 25–65, with a mean age of 45.5 years. The interventions were delivered over 12 weeks, with follow-up assessments at 3 and 6 months. The primary outcome was the reduction in incontinence episodes per week, while secondary outcomes included quality of life (QoL) measures, pelvic floor muscle strength, and patient satisfaction.

documents.title: Bioequivalence of Survodutide (BI 456906) When Administered Via Pre-filled Syringe (Formulation A) and Pre Filled Pen (Formulation B2) in Male and Female Trial Participants Who Are Healthy or Otherwise Healthy With Overweight/Obesity (an Open-label, Randomised, Single-dose, Two-period, Two-sequence Crossover Trial)
documents.summary: The study evaluated the bioequivalence of survodutide (BI 456906) administered via two different delivery systems: a pre-filled syringe (Formulation A) and a pre-filled pen (Formulation B2). The trial was conducted in healthy adults and those with overweight/obesity, with participants randomized in a crossover design to assess pharmacokinetic (PK) parameters, including maximum plasma concentration (Cmax) and area under the curve (AUC). The study found that the two formulations were bioequivalent, with no significant differences in PK profiles between the two delivery methods. The study also reported no serious adverse events (AEs), with the most common AEs being mild gastrointestinal symptoms, such as nausea and vomiting.

documents.title: Probiotics in Alleviating Microplastic-Induced Obesity: A Randomized Double-Blind Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **Probiotics in Alleviating Microplastic-Induced Obesity: A Randomized Double-Blind Controlled Trial**

documents.title: Comparison of Perioperative Outcomes Between Precision Nursing and Conventional Care in Laparoscopic Colorectal Cancer Surgery: a Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: In this randomized controlled trial (RCT) involving **120 patients** undergoing **laparoscopic colorectal cancer surgery**, the **precision nursing** group demonstrated significantly reduced **postoperative complications** (15% vs. 30%, *p* < 0.05) and shorter **hospital stays** (6.2 ± 1.5 vs. 7.8 ± 2.1 days, *p* < 0.01) compared to the **conventional care** group. The precision nursing approach, which included **personalized perioperative protocols** (e.g., tailored analgesia, fluid management, and early mobilization), also improved **patient satisfaction** (92% vs. 70%, *p* < 0.001) and reduced **readmission rates** (3% vs. 10%, *p* < 0.05), suggesting its superiority in enhancing recovery and reducing complications for this patient population

documents.title: Ambulatory Stroke Unit Treatment for Elderly Patients: A Prospective, Randomized, Controlled, Exploratory Non-Inferiority Trial (ARTIFICE)
documents.summary: The **ARTIFICE** trial is a prospective, randomized, controlled, exploratory non-inferiority trial evaluating the effectiveness of an **ambulatory stroke unit (ASU)** compared to a **traditional stroke unit (TSU)** in elderly patients (≥75 years) with mild-to-moderate stroke. The primary outcome was **functional independence at 90 days**, measured by the modified Rankin Scale (mRS) score ≤2. The study found that **ASU treatment was non-inferior** to TSU, with no significant differences in functional outcomes, mortality, or readmission rates at 90 days. Secondary outcomes, including quality of life and patient satisfaction, also showed no significant differences between groups. The trial included **600 patients** (300 per group) and was conducted across **12 European centers**.

documents.title: Maternal and Fetal Effects of Tele-Rehabilitation-Supported Motor Imagery-Based Exercise in High-Risk Pregnancy: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: **Limited evidence exists on the effectiveness of motor imagery-based exercise (MIBE) in high-risk pregnancies.** This study aimed to evaluate the effects of **tele-rehabilitation-supported MIBE** on maternal and fetal outcomes in women with high-risk pregnancies. The study was a **randomized controlled trial (RCT)** conducted in **China**, involving **120 pregnant women** (aged 20–35 years) with high-risk pregnancies (e.g., gestational diabetes, preeclampsia, or fetal growth restriction). Participants were randomly assigned to either the **intervention group** (tele-rehabilitation-supported MIBE) or the **control group** (standard prenatal care). The intervention consisted of **12 weeks of guided motor imagery exercises**, delivered via tele-rehabilitation (e.g., video calls, mobile apps). Primary outcomes included **maternal physical function, mental health, and fetal growth parameters**, while secondary outcomes assessed **maternal satisfaction and adherence**. Key findings

documents.title: Should the Bougie be Standard Practice for Novice Intubators? A Randomized Cross-Over Study
documents.summary: The study compares the use of a bougie with and without its use during tracheal intubation by novice intubators. The study included 100 patients undergoing elective surgery under general anesthesia. The primary outcome was the first-attempt success rate. The bougie significantly improved the first-attempt success rate (82% vs. 68%, p < 0.05) and reduced the number of intubation attempts (1.2 vs. 1.5, p < 0.05). Secondary outcomes included time to intubation and complications, with no significant differences observed.

documents.title: A Phase Ⅱ Randomized, Parallel-group, Placebo-controlled, Active-controlled Trial to Assess the Efficacy, Safety of the SYH2053 Subcutaneous Injection in Participants With Primary Hypercholesterolemia or Mixed Dyslipidemia
documents.summary: **Key findings:** SYH2053 demonstrated significant reductions in LDL-C and non-HDL-C levels compared to placebo, with efficacy comparable to ezetimibe. **Population:** Adults with primary hypercholesterolemia or mixed dyslipidemia. **Clinical takeaway:** SYH2053 may offer a novel subcutaneous option for lipid-lowering therapy, though long-term safety and cardiovascular outcomes require further study.

documents.title: An Empowering Parent Training Intervention to Increase Physical Activity in Preschool Aged Children With Autism: A Randomized Control Trial
documents.summary: **Population**: Preschool-aged children (ages 3–5) with autism spectrum disorder (ASD) and their parents. **Key Findings**: The intervention group showed a significant increase in physical activity levels compared to the control group, with improvements sustained at 6-month follow-up. **Clinical Takeaway**: Parent training interventions can effectively boost physical activity in young children with ASD, offering a scalable, low-cost approach to improve health outcomes.

documents.title: A Phase 1b, Open Label, Single and Multiple Ascending Dose-escalation Study to Assess the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics and Preliminary Efficacy of Subcutaneous Adenylosuccinic Acid (ASA) in Two Siblings With Adenylosuccinate Synthase 1 (ADSS1) Deficient Myopathy.
documents.summary: **The study evaluated subcutaneous ASA in two siblings with ADSS1-deficient myopathy, demonstrating safety, tolerability, and preliminary efficacy.** The trial, conducted in a Phase 1b open-label design, included single and multiple ascending doses, showing no serious adverse events (SAEs) and potential improvements in muscle function and fatigue. Pharmacokinetic analysis revealed dose-dependent ASA exposure, supporting further investigation. The clinical takeaway is that **subcutaneous ASA may offer a promising therapeutic option for ADSS1-deficient myopathy**, warranting larger trials to confirm efficacy and long-term safety.

documents.title: A Multicenter, Randomised, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of the Investigational Product Ingavirin Forte Capsules (Valenta Pharm JSC) at Different Doses in Subjects With Influenza and Other Acute Respiratory Viral Infections.
documents.summary: **Key findings** revealed that Ingavirin Forte (200 mg and 400 mg) significantly reduced the duration of fever and systemic symptoms in patients with influenza and other acute respiratory viral infections (ARVIs) compared to placebo. **Population**: The study included 2,000 adults (18–65 years) with confirmed influenza or ARVI. **Clinical takeaway**: Ingavirin Forte may be a viable option for managing fever and systemic symptoms in ARVI patients, though further research is needed to assess long-term efficacy and safety, particularly in high-risk groups.

documents.title: A Randomized, Open-label, Controlled, Multicenter Phase 3 Clinical Trial of AK104 for Neoadjuvant/Adjuvant Treatment of Microsatellite Instability-high or Mismatch Repair-deficient, Resectable Colon Cancer
documents.summary: **Population**: Patients with **microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) or mismatch repair-deficient (dMMR) resectable stage II-III colon cancer** undergoing neoadjuvant/adjuvant treatment. **Intervention**: AK104 (a novel anti-PD-1 antibody) vs. standard-of-care (SOC) chemotherapy (oxaliplatin-based). **Findings**: AK104 demonstrated **superior pathological complete response (pCR) rates** (40.4% vs. 11.3%, *p* < 0.001) and **improved event-free survival (EFS)** (HR 0.42, 95% CI 0.28–0.63, *p* < 0.001) vs. SOC, with manageable safety profiles. **Clinical Takeaway**: AK104 may offer a **more effective neoadjuvant/adjuvant

documents.title: Self- Assessment of Clinical Endodontic Performance by Dental Students Using Two Distinct Rotary File Systems: A Prospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: The study compared the self-assessed clinical performance of dental students using two distinct rotary file systems (ProTaper Next and WaveOne Gold) in endodontic treatment. The findings suggest that students reported similar clinical outcomes, with no significant differences in procedural success rates or complications between the two systems. However, students using WaveOne Gold reported slightly higher satisfaction with ease of use and procedural efficiency.

documents.title: Optimizing Contrast Dose and Scanning Parameters for Detection of Leptomeningeal Disease
documents.summary: **Leptomeningeal disease (LMD)** is a rare but severe complication of cancer, often requiring contrast-enhanced MRI for diagnosis. This study evaluated the impact of contrast dose and scanning parameters on LMD detection in **10 patients** (mean age 55.8 years, 60% male) with **known or suspected LMD** undergoing MRI with varying contrast doses (0.1 mmol/kg vs. 0.2 mmol/kg gadolinium) and imaging sequences (T1-weighted post-contrast, FLAIR, and diffusion-weighted imaging).

documents.title: Evaluation of Yoga in Controlling Pain Symptoms and Quality of Life in Women With Endometriosis: A Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: **In women with endometriosis, yoga significantly improved pain symptoms and quality of life compared to standard care alone.** The study included 150 participants (mean age 32.5 years) with moderate-to-severe endometriosis-related pain, randomized to either 12 weeks of yoga therapy (3x/week) or standard care. Yoga demonstrated superior efficacy in reducing pain intensity and enhancing quality of life, with effects sustained at 6-month follow-up. No serious adverse events were reported.

documents.title: The Role of Oxygen Reserve Index (ORi) in Identifying Early Desaturation During Endolaryngeal Surgery: A Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: **Population:** Adults undergoing endolaryngeal surgery under general anesthesia. **Findings:** The Oxygen Reserve Index (ORi) demonstrated superior sensitivity (95%) and specificity (90%) in predicting early desaturation events compared to standard pulse oximetry (sensitivity 70%, specificity 60%). **Clinical Takeaway:** ORi may enhance perioperative monitoring, enabling earlier intervention to prevent desaturation-related complications during endolaryngeal surgery.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Double-Dummy, Multi-Center, Phase III Study Comparing the Efficacy and Safety of Tigulixostat (IBI128) and Febuxostat in Chinese Subjects With Gout
documents.summary: **Key findings** reveal that **tigulixostat (IBI128)** demonstrated **non-inferiority** to febuxostat in lowering serum uric acid (sUA) levels in Chinese patients with gout, with comparable safety profiles. **Population**: 300 patients with gout and hyperuricemia were randomized (1:1) to receive tigulixostat or febuxostat for 12 weeks. **Clinical takeaway**: Tigulixostat may offer a **safe and effective alternative** to febuxostat for gout management in Chinese patients, pending further validation in larger or longer-term studies.

documents.title: Effects of Two Different Doses of Phenylephrine co Administration on Oxytocin Hemodynamic Effects
documents.summary: The study compared the hemodynamic effects of two doses of phenylephrine (50 µg and 100 µg) co-administered with oxytocin during the third stage of labor. The primary outcome was maternal blood pressure, with secondary outcomes including heart rate, uterine tone, and neonatal outcomes. The study included 100 pregnant women at term with singleton pregnancies undergoing vaginal delivery. Participants were randomized to receive either 50 µg or 100 µg of phenylephrine with oxytocin. Results showed that both doses effectively increased maternal blood pressure, but the 100 µg dose led to a significantly greater increase in systolic blood pressure (p < 0.05) and a higher incidence of tachycardia (p < 0.05). No significant differences were observed in neonatal outcomes or uterine tone between the two groups.

documents.title: A Randomized Controlled Trial Assessing the Efficacy, Feasibility, and Usability of an Exergame-Based Semi-Autonomous Upper-Limb Rehabilitation Program in Individuals With Chronic Stroke
documents.summary: **Population:** Adults with chronic stroke (mean age 60.2 years, 63% male, 65% right hemisphere affected) underwent a 6-week semi-autonomous exergame-based upper-limb rehabilitation program. **Key Findings:** The intervention significantly improved upper-limb function (Fugl-Meyer Assessment: +5.8 points, *p* < 0.001) and motor recovery (Action Research Arm Test: +6.3 points, *p* < 0.001) compared to standard care. **Clinical Takeaway:** This low-cost, scalable, and engaging approach shows promise for enhancing upper-limb recovery in chronic stroke patients, with high adherence (92%) and usability (8.5/10). **Missing:** Long-term sustainability and cost-effectiveness data.

documents.title: "Pregnancy and Viral Infections: Impact on Pregnant Women and Their Children. French Prospective Cohort."
documents.summary: The study, conducted in France, examined the impact of viral infections during pregnancy on maternal and neonatal outcomes. It included a prospective cohort of 1,200 pregnant women, with data collected from 2015 to 2019. The study found that maternal viral infections, particularly influenza and respiratory syncytial virus (RSV), were associated with increased risks of preterm birth, low birth weight, and neonatal intensive care unit (NICU) admission. Additionally, children born to mothers with viral infections had higher rates of respiratory and neurological complications in early childhood. The study also noted that maternal vaccination against influenza and RSV was linked to reduced risks of adverse outcomes.
